section 1--introduction 

video 2

0
1
00:00:00,190 --> 00:00:04,180
Alright. Now let's understand what exactly is a computer program.
1

2
00:00:04,290 --> 00:00:12,390
A program is a series of instructions that cause a computer or a microcontroller to perform a particular
2

3
00:00:12,390 --> 00:00:17,540
task. A computer program includes more than just instructions.
3

4
00:00:17,550 --> 00:00:25,830
It also contains data and various memory addresses on which the instructions work to perform a specific
4

5
00:00:25,830 --> 00:00:26,670
task.
5

6
00:00:26,670 --> 00:00:32,480
Now, let's look at different programming languages which we use in embedded systems design.
6

7
00:00:33,090 --> 00:00:37,790
So, to write a program we have to use programming language, isn't it?
7

8
00:00:37,860 --> 00:00:45,300
Note that, if your goal is to become an embedded system programmer, then you must be good at C or C++
8

9
00:00:45,300 --> 00:00:47,600
programming languages or both.
9

10
00:00:47,870 --> 00:00:54,510
Nowadays, a rust programming language also has quickly gained popularity and also becoming very much
10

11
00:00:54,510 --> 00:00:56,890
famous in embedded programming.
11

12
00:00:56,970 --> 00:01:00,120
C, C++, Rust, Assembly,
12

13
00:01:00,150 --> 00:01:05,310
so these are the main programming languages which are being used in embedded systems.
13

14
00:01:05,370 --> 00:01:12,660
The usage of Python, Micro Python, and Java are also rarely used in embedded systems code development.
14

15
00:01:12,980 --> 00:01:17,790
Assembly level programming is very much powerful and it is still being used in various parts of the
15

16
00:01:17,810 --> 00:01:18,960
embedded project.
16

17
00:01:19,170 --> 00:01:26,030
So, it's not like you know a C and C++ have completely taken over the assembly level programming. A still
17

18
00:01:26,060 --> 00:01:30,370
some part of the project is coded in assembly level programming.
18

19
00:01:30,380 --> 00:01:35,450
Now, if you look at the survey of 2017 made by Embedded.com,
19

20
00:01:35,480 --> 00:01:40,680
so here is a screenshot of Dan Saks presentation during the keynotes speech.
20

21
00:01:40,700 --> 00:01:46,120
So, this video you can find on you tube. So, very much informative video you should watch it on YouTube.
21

22
00:01:46,250 --> 00:01:52,280
If you look at this slide, you can see that 'C' is still the number 1 and highly favored programming
22

23
00:01:52,280 --> 00:01:59,630
language for embedded systems followed by C++ and assembly. But you can also notice that usage of 'C'
23

24
00:01:59,750 --> 00:02:06,800
may be declining. So, in future C++ and Rust programming languages also have the potential to gain
24

25
00:02:06,800 --> 00:02:13,800
significant popularity in embedded systems programming. But still 'C' leads in embedded domain.
25

26
00:02:13,800 --> 00:02:19,980
It is one of the most powerful high level programming languages to write programs for embedded systems.
26

27
00:02:20,340 --> 00:02:22,740
So, you can download this survey as a PDF
27

28
00:02:22,860 --> 00:02:25,990
if you want from Embedded.com website.
28

29
00:02:26,120 --> 00:02:31,540
Alright. So, that gives us a motivation why we should learn 'C' programming language, because it is still
29

30
00:02:31,540 --> 00:02:35,800
one of the leading programming languages for embedded systems.
30

31
00:02:35,850 --> 00:02:40,200
Now let's get started with exploring the 'C' programming language.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

video 3

0
1
00:00:00,120 --> 00:00:07,530
Let's look at 'C' programming standardization and will also look at some history of 'C' programming language.
1

2
00:00:07,530 --> 00:00:13,700
The 'C' Programming language was initially developed in AT&T labs by Professor Brian Kernighan and Dennis
2

3
00:00:13,710 --> 00:00:16,210
Ritchie, known as K&R C.
3

4
00:00:16,230 --> 00:00:21,400
So, during the year 1970, 'C' programming became very much popular
4

5
00:00:21,450 --> 00:00:27,780
but without any serious standardization to the language. The non-official standard was called K&R
5

6
00:00:27,780 --> 00:00:32,520
C, which led to many ambiguities among different compiler programmers,
6

7
00:00:32,520 --> 00:00:35,580
and that actually led to non-portable codes.
7

8
00:00:35,810 --> 00:00:43,950
K&R C was a first non-official 'C' standard. So, in 1989 American National Standards Institute(ANSI)  
8

9
00:00:43,950 --> 00:00:49,750
designed and approved first official 'C' standard, which is also denoted by this tag name.
9

10
00:00:50,270 --> 00:00:56,410
And in 1990 it was approved by ISO as international standard for 'C' programming language.
10

11
00:00:56,420 --> 00:01:03,310
So the ISO tag name is this one. So, that means the first official international standard was released
11

12
00:01:03,340 --> 00:01:05,520
by ISO in 1990.
12

13
00:01:05,530 --> 00:01:15,830
So that's why, it is also called as ANSI C or C89 or C90 standard.So, the language underwent few more
13

14
00:01:15,830 --> 00:01:24,020
changes (like addition of new features, addition of new syntaxes, data types etc.) and newly updated
14

15
00:01:24,020 --> 00:01:28,480
standard was released in 1999 under the ISO tag
15

16
00:01:28,520 --> 00:01:39,010
this one, which is also called C99 standard in short. So, in December 2011 ISO released new
16

17
00:01:39,010 --> 00:01:46,430
standard under this tag name, which is also called as 
C11 standard. C11 supersedes the C99
17

18
00:01:46,430 --> 00:01:47,610
standard.
18

19
00:01:47,620 --> 00:01:53,350
So now in this course, we will be using the C11 standard which is actually compiler default with
19

20
00:01:53,350 --> 00:01:55,600
some compiler extensions.
20

21
00:01:55,600 --> 00:02:01,210
So, collectively we call it as a gnu11. So more on this, we'll see later. How to change the compiler
21

22
00:02:01,210 --> 00:02:04,270
settings to mention the 'C' standard?
22

23
00:02:04,480 --> 00:02:06,430
So, we'll cover this later.
23

24
00:02:06,430 --> 00:02:13,450
So, note that if you have written a code using C90 standard, then it will compile without any issues
24

25
00:02:13,540 --> 00:02:19,480
in C99 standard compilation, so that what we call is backward compatibility.
25

26
00:02:19,480 --> 00:02:26,050
So but if you have written code using C99 standard specific features, then it may not compile
26

27
00:02:26,050 --> 00:02:30,090
successfully in C90 compilation. So, you should keep this in mind. 
27

28
00:02:30,190 --> 00:02:35,410
But don't worry about this, so we'll see how to set the standard in the compiler later. From the next
28

29
00:02:35,410 --> 00:02:36,190
lecture onwards
29

30
00:02:36,190 --> 00:02:38,130
let's get started with programming.
30

31
00:02:38,260 --> 00:02:41,830
So, let's start with the Hello world program and I'll see you in the next lecture.


==================================================================================

section 2--IDE installation

video 1

1
1

00:00:00,000  -->  00:00:07,039
In this video, let's understand about installing an IDE i.e. Integrated Development Environment.
2

2

00:00:07,599  -->  00:00:13,600
IDE is a software that contains all the essential tools to develop, compile, link,
3

3

00:00:13,600  -->  00:00:20,879
and debug your code. In some cases, you may have to integrate compiler and debugger tools to the
4

4

00:00:20,879  -->  00:00:28,319
IDE manually. So, throughout this course, we will be using Eclipse-based STM32Qube IDE,
5

5

00:00:28,399  -->  00:00:35,439
which is developed by STMicroelectronics to write, compile, debug your embedded system applications
6

6

00:00:35,439  -->  00:00:44,479
on STM32 ARM-based microcontrollers. STM32Qube IDE is an Eclipse-based IDE with STM32-related
7

7

00:00:44,479  -->  00:00:50,799
customization. Alright, so now let's see how to download this software STM32Qube IDE. So,
8

8

00:00:50,799  -->  00:00:57,680
just search in the Google and go to the official website of ST. And when you are here,
9

9

00:00:58,240  -->  00:01:05,120
okay, you can just browse this web page and here you can download as per your machine. So,
10

10

00:01:05,120  -->  00:01:11,760
since I'm using Windows machine, I will download for Windows. Okay, and here select the version.
11

11

00:01:11,760  -->  00:01:18,160
Alright, so a small note on which version of STM32Qube IDE to use. So, tool versions keep
12

12

00:01:18,160  -->  00:01:25,040
changing over time and our goal is not to chase every new release. Our goal is to learn embedded
13

13

00:01:25,040  -->  00:01:32,959
systems, right, and to build projects smoothly. So, ST has recently released STM32Qube IDE version
14

14

00:01:32,959  -->  00:01:41,440
2.0. So, in this IDE, so the QBMX code generator has been separated from the IDE. That means you
15

15

00:01:41,440  -->  00:01:47,760
now need to install and manage two separate tools. Okay, if you need code generator or code
16

16

00:01:47,760  -->  00:01:54,239
configurator, then you need to install QBMX separately and the overall workflow is a bit
17

17

00:01:54,239  -->  00:02:00,559
different. Okay, actually we don't use QBMX code generation for this course because we write
18

18

00:02:00,559  -->  00:02:07,599
everything from scratch and we focus on bare metal programming. But still, the newer version of
19

19

00:02:07,599  -->  00:02:17,039
STM32Qube IDE has introduced the UI and the workflow changes that can confuse. Okay, and
20

20

00:02:17,039  -->  00:02:23,919
it could make bit harder to follow the code steps exactly. Okay, so for this course, it's better you
21

21

00:02:23,919  -->  00:02:33,520
stick to the STM32Qube IDE versions below 2.0. Okay, such as version 1.18 or 1.19. So, this will
22

22

00:02:33,520  -->  00:02:39,119
keep your setup consistent with the course demonstrations and it will be easier to follow
23

23

00:02:39,119  -->  00:02:45,119
every exercise without a tool related distractions. Alright, so I will select the version,
24

24

00:02:45,119  -->  00:02:56,639
let's say 1.19. Okay, and let's accept this license terms. Now, you can download as a guest
25

25

00:02:56,639  -->  00:03:03,279
or by logging into the MyST account. So, for that, you need to register first. So, I'll just download
26

26

00:03:03,279  -->  00:03:13,440
as guest and here just provide all this information. So, you can use your Gmail, no problem here.
27

27

00:03:14,000  -->  00:03:21,839
Okay, so once you do that, you will receive an email to download the software.
28

28

00:03:21,839  -->  00:03:27,119
So, I'll just click download now and the download starts automatically.
29

29

00:03:30,160  -->  00:03:33,600
So, download the software and I'll see you in the next video.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

video 2

0
1
00:00:00,060 --> 00:00:06,360
Let's see some of the features of this STM32CubeIDE. In this web page they have listed
1

2
00:00:06,420 --> 00:00:08,860
some of the key features of this IDE.
2

3
00:00:09,090 --> 00:00:12,890
Let's understand what are the features which are important to us.
3

4
00:00:13,020 --> 00:00:17,490
The very important thing is, it supports all the OS platforms.
4

5
00:00:17,490 --> 00:00:17,880
Why?
5

6
00:00:17,880 --> 00:00:20,040
Because, it is based on Eclipse.
6

7
00:00:20,040 --> 00:00:21,820
That's why, Multi-OS support.
7

8
00:00:21,840 --> 00:00:23,790
So that's the good news.
8

9
00:00:23,790 --> 00:00:30,600
And the next important thing is, it comes with inbuilt cross compilers and debuggers.
9

10
00:00:30,600 --> 00:00:37,300
You need not to download any external compilers or debuggers to integrate that with this IDE. So,
10

11
00:00:37,470 --> 00:00:40,020
everything comes in one single bundle.
11

12
00:00:40,110 --> 00:00:49,110
So, this IDE actually uses a GNU based C C++ compiler for ARM embedded processors.It has a several other
12

13
00:00:49,110 --> 00:00:55,830
features like importing projects from Atollic, TrueSTUDIO, and System Workbench. And also in this IDE
13

14
00:00:55,970 --> 00:01:03,260
you find some of the advanced debug features including SWV, which stands for a System analysis and real
14

15
00:01:03,260 --> 00:01:09,780
time tracing which we will be using in this course. And I'll be explaining how to use SWV in the later part
15

16
00:01:09,780 --> 00:01:12,250
of the course, and also of course you can
16

17
00:01:12,240 --> 00:01:20,160
observe all the CPU core registers, IP registers, and also you can browse various memory locations of the
17

18
00:01:20,160 --> 00:01:25,000
microcontroller a while you debug the code. So, everything will be covered in this course.
18

19
00:01:25,740 --> 00:01:34,040
And another important feature of this IDE is it has inbuilt support for STM32CubeMX.
19

20
00:01:34,050 --> 00:01:39,870
Now earlier we used to install a separate STM32CubeMX software and then we used to generate
20

21
00:01:39,900 --> 00:01:40,440
the code
21

22
00:01:40,440 --> 00:01:44,640
and after that we used to import that project into the eclipse software.
22

23
00:01:45,060 --> 00:01:48,630
But with the release of this IDE that is not required.
23

24
00:01:49,080 --> 00:01:52,020
So, it gives inbuilt support for 
24

25
00:01:52,050 --> 00:01:59,810
STM32CubeMX a for peripheral configuration and code generation, but this feature that is integrated  
25

26
00:01:59,830 --> 00:02:03,590
STM32CubeMX feature will not be used in this course.
26

27
00:02:03,730 --> 00:02:06,630
So, you should remember that will not be using STM32CubeMX.

1
00:00:00,090 --> 00:00:06,300
So, in this lecture let's understand installation of GNU Compiler Collections for host.
1

2
00:00:06,300 --> 00:00:10,210
That means, we are going to install the GCC compiler for host.
2

3
00:00:10,590 --> 00:00:17,730
So, note that the STM32CubeIDE which we installed in the previous video is an integrated development
3

4
00:00:17,760 --> 00:00:25,420
environment software to develop, compile, and debug embedded applications for STM32 microcontrollers.
4

5
00:00:25,680 --> 00:00:31,860
So, the STM32CubeIDE installation procedure which we carried out in the previous video, also
5

6
00:00:31,860 --> 00:00:36,790
installed the GCC cross compiler for ARM Cortex Mx processors.
6

7
00:00:36,870 --> 00:00:42,510
So, you need not to download any external cross compilers. It is already done. In this course,
7

8
00:00:42,540 --> 00:00:48,120
we are using a same IDE, to write and compile 'C' programs for our host machine. 
8

9
00:00:48,150 --> 00:00:50,890
That is Windows, Linux, and Mac machines.
9

10
00:00:50,970 --> 00:00:57,190
So now we need to install the compiler for our host machine, which doesn't come with the  
10

11
00:00:57,200 --> 00:01:00,150
STM32CubeIDE installation. So, that we are going to do now.
11

12
00:01:01,220 --> 00:01:07,370
If you are using Linux or Mac, then it is very simple you just have to run few commands in order to install
12

13
00:01:07,370 --> 00:01:09,700
the GCC compiler for your host.
13

14
00:01:09,860 --> 00:01:17,510
So that I am going to cover later, but in the next lecture I'm going to cover how to install GCC for your
14

15
00:01:17,510 --> 00:01:18,530
Windows machine.
15

16
00:01:18,560 --> 00:01:26,750
It is not that straightforward because, we use GCC in windows through a tool called MinGW. MinGW stands
16

17
00:01:26,750 --> 00:01:30,280
for Minimalist GNU tools for Windows.
17

18
00:01:30,470 --> 00:01:34,190
So, it is actually the windows flavor of GNU tools.
18

19
00:01:34,700 --> 00:01:39,530
So, in the next lecture I'll be covering how to install MinGW on your Windows machine,
19

20
00:01:39,530 --> 00:01:43,810
so if you are reusing Linux or Mac machines, please skip the next video.

2
00:00:03,120 --> 00:00:09,690
So, I have given a link with this lecture, so you can find in the resource section of this lecture.
2

3
00:00:09,690 --> 00:00:11,430
Please go through that link.
3

4
00:00:11,910 --> 00:00:18,710
So basically it's a Google drive link. So, there you will find a file called CTOOLS.zip.
4

5
00:00:18,750 --> 00:00:21,120
So, you do not to Sign In all anything, Ok?
5

6
00:00:21,480 --> 00:00:23,430
you can just download that file.
6

7
00:00:24,210 --> 00:00:26,010
So, just download this.
7

8
00:00:26,010 --> 00:00:33,930
And once you download this zip file, OK? actually you have to extract that into the C drive of your
8

9
00:00:34,470 --> 00:00:35,310
computer.
9

10
00:00:36,270 --> 00:00:37,320
So, here it is.
10

11
00:00:37,320 --> 00:00:43,080
And in CTOOLS, you will find two directories. bin and mingw32.
11

12
00:00:43,170 --> 00:00:51,660
So, basically it has all the binaries required to compile and manage the project (C project).
12

13
00:00:51,960 --> 00:00:53,550
So, I hope you can do that.
13

14
00:00:53,550 --> 00:01:02,460
And, so if you just open the command prompt and if you try to run the 'gcc' command, then it says "it is not
14

15
00:01:02,460 --> 00:01:03,930
a recognized command".
15

16
00:01:05,150 --> 00:01:11,930
If you try the remove command, which are essential to clean and build the projects.
16

17
00:01:12,470 --> 00:01:17,090
So but the system says "those commands are not recognized".
17

18
00:01:17,090 --> 00:01:23,690
And you may also required to run the 'make' command, and it is also not recognized.
18

19
00:01:24,580 --> 00:01:31,330
So, all these commands are actually there, the executables are actually there in these folders.
19

20
00:01:32,530 --> 00:01:37,360
So now, we have to configure these paths into the System
20

21
00:01:37,360 --> 00:01:38,890
Environment variables.
21

22
00:01:39,690 --> 00:01:40,710
So, for that,
22

23
00:01:40,740 --> 00:01:48,750
just type env in the Windows search bar and you will get Edit the system environment variables.
23

24
00:01:48,750 --> 00:01:54,300
So, just open that, OK?  And go to Environment variables,
24

25
00:01:55,190 --> 00:01:57,230
and here go to Path,
25

26
00:01:58,040 --> 00:02:00,140
Edit, New.
26

27
00:02:00,830 --> 00:02:02,810
So, first let's give this Path.
27

28
00:02:03,680 --> 00:02:05,180
So, just copy this
28

29
00:02:08,710 --> 00:02:11,410
and you can paste here.
29

30
00:02:12,430 --> 00:02:13,690
And after that,
30

31
00:02:15,060 --> 00:02:16,770
get inside the mingw32,
31

32
00:02:16,800 --> 00:02:24,570
get inside bin, and copy this path, and click New and paste here.
32

33
00:02:24,600 --> 00:02:25,350
Now click
33

34
00:02:25,350 --> 00:02:26,010
OK.
34

35
00:02:27,090 --> 00:02:27,870
OK
35

36
00:02:27,870 --> 00:02:29,970
and OK here. And now
36

37
00:02:29,970 --> 00:02:37,770
let's close this command prompt. And let's run the command prompt once again, and now type 'gcc'
37

38
00:02:37,770 --> 00:02:40,320
and it recognizes the command. OK?
38

39
00:02:40,590 --> 00:02:43,800
It says 'gcc is recognized'.
39

40
00:02:44,010 --> 00:02:48,810
I mean, it may throw this error, but that's not a problem.
40

41
00:02:48,810 --> 00:02:51,270
So, the windows recognized that command. OK?
41

42
00:02:51,570 --> 00:02:55,180
Similarly, you can run 'rm' or 'make'. OK?
42

43
00:02:55,200 --> 00:02:56,970
So, now everything is recognized.
43

44
00:02:56,970 --> 00:03:04,190
We have successfully installed the 'gcc' command, which is required to compile our C project.


━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

video 7

0
1
00:00:00,220 --> 00:00:05,430
Alright. So, now a installing GCC on your Linux machine is very very simple.
1

2
00:00:05,820 --> 00:00:06,100
OK.
2

3
00:00:06,110 --> 00:00:07,660
Here I am using Ubuntu machine, 
3

4
00:00:07,660 --> 00:00:17,120
so just go to your command prompt and then type a sudo apt update.
4

5
00:00:17,250 --> 00:00:17,560
All right.
5

6
00:00:17,560 --> 00:00:21,790
So, please be patient until it finishes this command
6

7
00:00:25,600 --> 00:00:25,850
OK.
7

8
00:00:25,860 --> 00:00:31,290
After that just type sudo apt install build-essential.
8

9
00:00:31,950 --> 00:00:38,860
OK. So, this is going to install all the essential tools for the build system including the GCC compiler.
9

10
00:00:38,940 --> 00:00:39,320
OK.
10

11
00:00:40,430 --> 00:00:46,050
And here you can see that in my case it is showing a build essential is already the newest version.
11

12
00:00:46,580 --> 00:00:48,210
Because, I have already install it.
12

13
00:00:48,210 --> 00:00:48,430
OK
13

14
00:00:48,450 --> 00:00:49,230
in my machine.
14

15
00:00:49,310 --> 00:00:53,500
But for you this may not be the case if you are trying for the first time.
15

16
00:00:53,750 --> 00:00:59,570
So, for you it would ask you to  type y or n to proceed with the installation
16

17
00:00:59,570 --> 00:01:01,610
in that case you have to type y.
17

18
00:01:02,180 --> 00:01:07,380
So then, the installation will begin and it will install all the required tools.
18

19
00:01:07,400 --> 00:01:08,710
I hope you can do that,
19

20
00:01:08,840 --> 00:01:15,260
and once the build essential installation is successful, and then you can run the command GCC 
20

21
00:01:15,260 --> 00:01:21,770
-- version.OK. A to know the version of the newly installed GCC. 
21

22
00:01:21,770 --> 00:01:22,520
OK.
22

23
00:01:22,790 --> 00:01:26,000
And I hope you can do that and I will see you in the next lecture.



━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

video 8

0
1
00:00:00,290 --> 00:00:00,650
All right.
1

2
00:00:00,660 --> 00:00:07,800
So, now in this lecture let's install host compiler that is GCC on Mac machine. To install GCC on Mac
2

3
00:00:07,800 --> 00:00:08,430
machine
3

4
00:00:08,430 --> 00:00:16,110
you first have to install Homebrew. For that, open the terminal and now open the web browser and type
4

5
00:00:16,170 --> 00:00:17,000
brew.sh.
5

6
00:00:17,020 --> 00:00:20,850
The here copy this command,
6

7
00:00:23,600 --> 00:00:27,860
and paste it onto the terminal and hit enter.
7

8
00:00:27,860 --> 00:00:29,750
So, it may ask you to enter the password.
8

9
00:00:29,790 --> 00:00:30,420
Okay.
9

10
00:00:31,600 --> 00:00:36,170
Now, this installs the homebrew packages on the machine. 
10

11
00:00:36,250 --> 00:00:39,480
Please be patient until the installation completes. OK.
11

12
00:00:41,730 --> 00:00:49,980
After that, to install GCC just type the command  brew install gcc and please wait for the completion
12

13
00:00:49,980 --> 00:00:58,760
of the installation. Then you can just type the command gcc to verify the gcc and here we can see that
13

14
00:00:58,790 --> 00:01:02,530
gcc command is detected and it runs.
14

15
00:01:02,750 --> 00:01:06,620
That's how you install the host compiler GCC on the Mac machine.
15

16
00:01:06,680 --> 00:01:07,810
And I'll see you in the next lecture.


━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

video 9

0
1
00:00:00,090 --> 00:00:01,530
Hey, Welcome back to the lecture.
1

2
00:00:01,530 --> 00:00:07,770
So, in the previous lecture, we actually installed STM32CubeIDE, and in this lecture let's download
2

3
00:00:07,770 --> 00:00:15,030
all the source codes of this course and let's import all the projects into the STM32CubeIDE. So,
3

4
00:00:15,030 --> 00:00:16,320
this is very important step,
4

5
00:00:16,320 --> 00:00:18,090
I would suggest you to follow this.
5

6
00:00:18,300 --> 00:00:24,390
First, what you do is, in your machine create a folder called Embedded-C. You can create it anywhere
6

7
00:00:24,390 --> 00:00:28,410
you prefer, and in that folder create three more folders.
7

8
00:00:28,410 --> 00:00:30,300
The first one is 'instructor'.
8

9
00:00:30,360 --> 00:00:36,150
Here, you are going to save the source codes given by the instructor. And then create one more folder
9

10
00:00:36,150 --> 00:00:42,000
called My_workspace, where you are going to develop your applications as you make a progress in this
10

11
00:00:42,000 --> 00:00:47,580
course. And after that, you create one more folder called 'Downloads', where you are going to keep all
11

12
00:00:47,580 --> 00:00:52,530
the downloaded files or whatever you download software or whatever you download from the web, you keep
12

13
00:00:52,530 --> 00:00:53,760
that in 'downloads'.
13

14
00:00:54,150 --> 00:00:58,650
After that, what you have to do is, you have to head over to our repository.
14

15
00:00:58,680 --> 00:01:03,000
The repository link is given in the resource section of this lecture,
15

16
00:01:03,000 --> 00:01:10,800
you can just click that. And here, click on 'Clone or download', and then click on 'Download ZIP'.  And you
16

17
00:01:10,800 --> 00:01:13,410
are going to download this into Downloads folder.
17

18
00:01:13,410 --> 00:01:14,460
Let's do that.
18

19
00:01:15,930 --> 00:01:17,320
I just download it.
19

20
00:01:17,340 --> 00:01:19,380
So, now let's extract this.
20

21
00:01:19,380 --> 00:01:23,730
So, here you actually have all the source codes of this course.
21

22
00:01:25,510 --> 00:01:25,900
All right.
22

23
00:01:25,900 --> 00:01:32,620
So, after that, what you do is, you go inside the instructor folder and create two more empty folders
23

24
00:01:32,620 --> 00:01:34,840
called 'host' and 'target'.
24

25
00:01:36,120 --> 00:01:42,390
The reason for this is, we are going to keep all the projects which we write for our host machine we
25

26
00:01:42,390 --> 00:01:44,310
are going to keep in this folder.
26

27
00:01:45,380 --> 00:01:52,070
And whatever the projects we are going to write for our embedded target, we are going to keep in this
27

28
00:01:52,070 --> 00:01:53,090
target folder.
28

29
00:01:53,450 --> 00:01:56,690
So, create two more folders, host and target.
29

30
00:01:56,690 --> 00:02:02,410
And also, you should create the same folders under My_workspace also.
30

31
00:02:02,420 --> 00:02:04,760
So, here also create 'host' and 'target'.
31

32
00:02:05,000 --> 00:02:06,290
I hope you can do that.
32

33
00:02:06,290 --> 00:02:12,950
And after that, what you have to do is, you have to launch your STM32CubeIDE. So, let's launch it.
33

34
00:02:16,120 --> 00:02:21,670
So, here you can see that, it is asking you to Select a directory as workspace.
34

35
00:02:21,940 --> 00:02:28,360
So, what you have to do is, you have to go to your Embedded-C folder, go inside the instructor, go
35

36
00:02:28,360 --> 00:02:35,360
inside the host, and select this path, and come here and paste here.
36

37
00:02:35,380 --> 00:02:37,270
And now click on launch.
37

38
00:02:41,560 --> 00:02:48,340
So, now here you can see that, you actually created a Eclipse workspace called 'host', which is under
38

39
00:02:48,340 --> 00:02:49,300
instructor.
39

40
00:02:49,600 --> 00:02:56,150
So, now here you are going to keep all the projects which are written by the instructor for the host.
40

41
00:02:56,170 --> 00:03:00,430
What you have to do now is, go to File and click on Import.
41

42
00:03:01,250 --> 00:03:07,670
So, go to General, and here go to Existing projects into workspace,
42

43
00:03:08,120 --> 00:03:09,140
click Next.
43

44
00:03:09,410 --> 00:03:12,710
Here you have to give the directory(the root directory). For that,
44

45
00:03:12,710 --> 00:03:17,420
go to Embedded-C, Downloads, go to Embedded-C-master,
45

46
00:03:18,500 --> 00:03:19,560
All_source_codes,
46

47
00:03:19,580 --> 00:03:21,860
host. You just give this path.
47

48
00:03:23,860 --> 00:03:29,110
And after that, go back and paste here, and then click Browse.
48

49
00:03:29,710 --> 00:03:37,450
It will automatically select all the projects.And here, click this option, copy projects into workspace
49

50
00:03:37,450 --> 00:03:39,490
and then click on Finish.
50

51
00:03:45,600 --> 00:03:48,810
And after that, click on this icon here 'Restore'.
51

52
00:03:50,740 --> 00:03:57,230
So, here you can see that, all the instructor projects are imported into your local machine.
52

53
00:03:57,250 --> 00:03:59,770
So, here you can close this 'Information Center'.
53

54
00:03:59,770 --> 00:04:06,550
So, now you can also close this Build Analyzer, just close that Static_Stack_Analyzer, so you can close everything
54

55
00:04:06,550 --> 00:04:07,060
here.
55

56
00:04:07,570 --> 00:04:10,450
Now, we will try to compile this.
56

57
00:04:10,870 --> 00:04:13,540
So, let me select this first project
57

58
00:04:15,430 --> 00:04:18,040
and try to build this project.
58

59
00:04:18,040 --> 00:04:23,250
And you can see that, this project compiles by using a gcc. So,
59

60
00:04:24,250 --> 00:04:28,090
but we have not configured any gcc in this IDE, right?
60

61
00:04:28,090 --> 00:04:30,550
So, how it is getting that gcc?
61

62
00:04:31,710 --> 00:04:38,700
Now, the reason for that is, in my computer, it has detected some gcc maybe.
62

63
00:04:39,510 --> 00:04:47,520
So, if you just right-click on that project and if you go to the properties, and if you go to the C/C++
63

64
00:04:47,520 --> 00:04:50,310
Build, and if you go to the Settings.
64

65
00:04:52,570 --> 00:04:52,870
Not Settings.
65

66
00:04:53,620 --> 00:04:54,970
Go to Environment.
66

67
00:04:55,270 --> 00:05:01,610
And here you can see that, it has detected MINGW_HOME.
67

68
00:05:01,630 --> 00:05:07,720
So, as a result it is finding the gcc compiler.
68

69
00:05:07,750 --> 00:05:10,090
Now, let's try to clean this project.
69

70
00:05:13,460 --> 00:05:14,690
That also works.
70

71
00:05:15,140 --> 00:05:17,210
So, for you it may not be the case.
71

72
00:05:17,450 --> 00:05:22,550
You may see some red cross marks on these projects.
72

73
00:05:23,360 --> 00:05:26,180
So, what you have to do is, you have to set your preferences.
73

74
00:05:26,600 --> 00:05:38,360
You have to go to Window and go to Preferences, and here go to C/C++ and go to build, and go to Environment.
74

75
00:05:38,360 --> 00:05:40,850
So, here you define two variables.
75

76
00:05:40,850 --> 00:05:50,990
That is the first one is click on Add here, so MINGW_HOME and set its value to CTOOLS,
76

77
00:05:52,090 --> 00:05:54,250
mingw32, Right?
77

78
00:05:54,250 --> 00:06:01,840
So, that is our MINGW_HOME. Click OK. And again add another variable and let's call it as
78

79
00:06:02,820 --> 00:06:09,470
MSYS_HOME and its value is set to this path.
79

80
00:06:11,130 --> 00:06:13,140
Just CTOOLS.
80

81
00:06:15,160 --> 00:06:21,610
And then click on OK, Apply, Apply and Close.
81

82
00:06:22,670 --> 00:06:26,840
So, now you go to any project and you just check
82

83
00:06:30,270 --> 00:06:40,200
the projects C/C++ Build, and Environment section, and the values will be reflected here.
83

84
00:06:40,530 --> 00:06:43,840
Now, go to one of this a project here.
84

85
00:06:43,860 --> 00:06:49,580
Let's take the first project. Expand that. And right-click on that project and try to build that project.
85

86
00:06:49,590 --> 00:06:51,160
So, this is called compiling.
86

87
00:06:51,180 --> 00:06:54,930
So, just try to build. And check the console here.
87

88
00:06:54,930 --> 00:06:57,570
So, here you can see that, it builds successfully.
88

89
00:06:57,570 --> 00:07:04,380
The gcc is invoked and the project has been build successfully and you got the binary. So, you should
89

90
00:07:04,380 --> 00:07:07,440
not get any error here. If you want to clean this project,
90

91
00:07:07,440 --> 00:07:12,840
so you can click on 'Clean Project' here and it should clean up your project.
91

92
00:07:12,840 --> 00:07:16,170
So, that means, the binary which is generated got deleted.
92

93
00:07:16,200 --> 00:07:19,050
Atleast these two activities you should be able to do.
93

94
00:07:19,870 --> 00:07:26,350
So, now you just imported all the host projects written by the instructor of this course.
94

95
00:07:26,680 --> 00:07:32,320
Now, let's import all the Embedded Target projects written by the instructor.
95

96
00:07:32,950 --> 00:07:36,220
For that, what you have to do is, go to file,
96

97
00:07:36,310 --> 00:07:38,140
go to Switch Workspace.
97

98
00:07:38,410 --> 00:07:41,800
Click on Other. Here, instead of host
98

99
00:07:41,800 --> 00:07:43,410
you just mention the target.
99

100
00:07:43,420 --> 00:07:49,870
Or you can go to your Embedded-C folder and there you go inside the instructor, and if you go under the
100

101
00:07:49,870 --> 00:07:52,180
host, now you can see all the projects, isn't it? 
101

102
00:07:53,410 --> 00:07:54,880
You go under the target.
102

103
00:07:54,880 --> 00:07:55,150
So,
103

104
00:07:55,150 --> 00:07:56,380
instructor > target.
104

105
00:07:56,800 --> 00:08:01,390
So, just copy that and go to your eclipse and paste here,
105

106
00:08:02,770 --> 00:08:03,430
Launch.
106

107
00:08:04,360 --> 00:08:10,510
This is a new workspace called 'target', where we are going to keep all the projects of the
107

108
00:08:10,510 --> 00:08:11,590
Embedded target.
108

109
00:08:14,180 --> 00:08:17,000
So, this is a new workspace called target.
109

110
00:08:17,030 --> 00:08:18,470
Again, the same procedure.
110

111
00:08:18,650 --> 00:08:24,120
Go to file > Import > General > Existing projects >
111

112
00:08:24,140 --> 00:08:28,250
Next > Go to downloads > Embedded-C-master >
112

113
00:08:29,030 --> 00:08:30,230
All_source_codes > 
113

114
00:08:30,260 --> 00:08:31,490
go to target >
114

115
00:08:31,700 --> 00:08:36,040
and inside here go to stm32f407_disc.
115

116
00:08:36,050 --> 00:08:37,380
So, these are the projects.
116

117
00:08:37,400 --> 00:08:42,020
Copy this path, and then go to Eclipse paste here.
117

118
00:08:42,050 --> 00:08:53,570
Click on Browse, and click on Copy projects into workspace, and then Finish. And then click on Restore,
118

119
00:08:54,230 --> 00:08:56,240
and then close this,
119

120
00:08:56,240 --> 00:08:57,350
Close this.
120

121
00:08:58,220 --> 00:09:02,970
Here you can see that, these are the projects which are written for the Embedded target.
121

122
00:09:02,990 --> 00:09:04,610
There are more projects to come.
122

123
00:09:04,610 --> 00:09:08,750
So for I have just uploaded these projects into the repository.
123

124
00:09:08,810 --> 00:09:10,460
Let's try compiling.
124

125
00:09:10,640 --> 00:09:20,300
So, now let's select one project and then right-click on that and click on Build. And go to Console here.
125

126
00:09:20,300 --> 00:09:21,770
And here you can see that,
126

127
00:09:22,850 --> 00:09:25,460
the project is cross compiled, isn't it?
127

128
00:09:25,820 --> 00:09:33,030
So, now here you can see that, the compiler invoked is arm-none-eabi-gcc, so that's a cross compiler.
128

129
00:09:33,050 --> 00:09:38,960
So, now the code is built for the target. So, don't worry all these things you will learn when you create
129

130
00:09:38,960 --> 00:09:43,280
the project. So, the build was successful and now try to clean that.
130

131
00:09:44,650 --> 00:09:46,900
And the clean was also successful.
131

132
00:09:46,990 --> 00:09:49,150
You can see that the binary got deleted.
132

133
00:09:49,600 --> 00:09:54,950
So, now you just imported all the projects created by the instructor.
133

134
00:09:54,970 --> 00:10:02,270
So, now the instructor folder has all host projects as well as target projects.
134

135
00:10:02,290 --> 00:10:06,230
So, do not change any code in the instructor folder.
135

136
00:10:06,250 --> 00:10:06,650
Why?
136

137
00:10:06,670 --> 00:10:08,270
Because, it is given by instructor.
137

138
00:10:08,290 --> 00:10:10,060
So, this is for your reference.
138

139
00:10:10,060 --> 00:10:14,920
So, if you have any issues while you're working on the project, then you can come back to this folder
139

140
00:10:14,920 --> 00:10:19,870
and you can check the code, or you can launch the workspace and you can build the code.
140

141
00:10:20,230 --> 00:10:23,290
So, but do not try to change the projects.
141

142
00:10:23,320 --> 00:10:25,000
Do not try to edit this.
142

143
00:10:25,950 --> 00:10:30,000
So, you create your own project in the My_workspace folder.
143

144
00:10:30,150 --> 00:10:37,140
So, here you create the project for Host, and here you create the project for the target as instructed
144

145
00:10:37,140 --> 00:10:38,010
in the course.
145

146
00:10:38,340 --> 00:10:43,800
So, for that, let's create two more workspace under host and Target.
146

147
00:10:43,800 --> 00:10:48,990
For that, what you have to do is, you have to go to the Eclipse again, and again
147

148
00:10:48,990 --> 00:10:51,560
switch the workspace (Switch Workspace),
148

149
00:10:51,570 --> 00:10:55,040
let's click on Other, and instead of 'instructor'
149

150
00:10:55,050 --> 00:10:57,130
now you give My_workspace here.
150

151
00:10:57,150 --> 00:10:57,860
That's it.
151

152
00:10:57,870 --> 00:11:01,710
So, go to host, and copy this path,
152

153
00:11:02,040 --> 00:11:04,080
paste here, Launch.
153

154
00:11:05,150 --> 00:11:08,090
This is your workspace for the host.
154

155
00:11:10,790 --> 00:11:15,500
So, whenever you want to create a project you create here, so that will be taught in the course.
155

156
00:11:16,250 --> 00:11:19,850
And after that, let's again create one more workspace
156

157
00:11:20,760 --> 00:11:22,920
for My_workspace\target.
157

158
00:11:26,090 --> 00:11:27,950
So, this is your target workspace.
158

159
00:11:27,950 --> 00:11:33,040
So, what are the project you want to create for your target, you create here as taught in the course.
159

160
00:11:33,050 --> 00:11:35,780
I'm going to close this Eclipse now for a time being.
160

161
00:11:35,780 --> 00:11:39,440
Let's close this IDE.  And let's go back to our folder.
161

162
00:11:39,440 --> 00:11:42,590
So, this is for your reference and this is your working area.
162

163
00:11:43,610 --> 00:11:45,890
So, in the next lecture, let's see how to create a project.
163

164
00:11:45,890 --> 00:11:46,790
I'll see you in the next lecture.


━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

video 10

0
1
00:00:00,090 --> 00:00:06,620
So, in this lecture let me show you how to create a project using STM32CubeIDE. In this course
1

2
00:00:06,630 --> 00:00:09,950
sometimes you will be creating a project for our host.
2

3
00:00:09,990 --> 00:00:13,540
That means the project which is going to run on our host machine.
3

4
00:00:13,980 --> 00:00:19,370
So, in my case host machine is X86 64 bit machine.
4

5
00:00:19,380 --> 00:00:27,240
And sometimes we are going to create project for our target, and the target in our case is STM32 microcontroller 
5

6
00:00:27,240 --> 00:00:29,430
based Discovery board.
6

7
00:00:29,430 --> 00:00:33,360
Now let's first learn how to create a project for the host.
7

8
00:00:33,360 --> 00:00:40,720
For that, I am going to launch the STM32CubeIDE the same IDE you are going to use, and here
8

9
00:00:40,870 --> 00:00:46,150
we are going to open the workspace which we created for our host.
9

10
00:00:46,300 --> 00:00:51,700
So, let me type host here, My_workspace host. Let's launch.
10

11
00:00:59,300 --> 00:01:09,050
So, go to file and go to new and then click on C/C++ project, and click on C manage build here, and then
11

12
00:01:09,170 --> 00:01:15,630
you can click next, and then you can give the project name here.
12

13
00:01:15,660 --> 00:01:16,820
So, let me give the name
13

14
00:01:16,820 --> 00:01:17,640
000
14

15
00:01:17,640 --> 00:01:20,490
let's say, a TestProject.
15

16
00:01:20,490 --> 00:01:21,900
So, this is a project name
16

17
00:01:21,930 --> 00:01:30,540
and here select MinGW GCC. So, if you are creating the project under windows machine, or you can
17

18
00:01:30,540 --> 00:01:37,930
just select GCC in case you are using Linux or Mac based systems. Since, mine is windows
18

19
00:01:37,930 --> 00:01:43,910
I'm going to select MinGW GCC. Do not select MCU ARM or cross GCC. Why?
19

20
00:01:43,920 --> 00:01:52,060
Because, we are writing the application for our host. And after that you can click next and finish.So,
20

21
00:01:52,060 --> 00:01:54,580
here we can see that project is created.
21

22
00:01:54,720 --> 00:02:00,670
Let's add a source file. For that, right click and go to new source file.
22

23
00:02:01,060 --> 00:02:04,360
Let me add a source file let me call it as main.c.
23

24
00:02:04,390 --> 00:02:10,250
So, this is a source file, where I am going to write my 'C' program. Here it is.
24

25
00:02:10,250 --> 00:02:14,510
I just created a main.c file and here we are going to write the code.
25

26
00:02:14,570 --> 00:02:19,420
Let me quickly add some code so you need not to understand this code.
26

27
00:02:19,560 --> 00:02:24,040
So, now I just added a small code snippet into the source file
27

28
00:02:24,040 --> 00:02:24,980
main.c.
28

29
00:02:25,090 --> 00:02:27,090
So, you need not to understand this code anyway
29

30
00:02:27,110 --> 00:02:31,170
we are going to learn the 'C' programming from scratch in this course.
30

31
00:02:31,170 --> 00:02:36,010
So, I just added some code in order to show you how to build and test the code.
31

32
00:02:36,210 --> 00:02:38,540
Once you write your code into the source file.
32

33
00:02:38,640 --> 00:02:46,170
So, what you have to do is, you have to build the project. For that, righ t click on the project and then select, build
33

34
00:02:46,170 --> 00:02:49,750
project. And then check the console window here.
34

35
00:02:49,800 --> 00:02:52,310
So, it will show you all the build logs.
35

36
00:02:52,320 --> 00:02:54,900
For example, let me do it once again.
36

37
00:02:55,170 --> 00:03:01,780
So, let me build the project and here we can see that, the project is build successfully and the exe
37

38
00:03:01,800 --> 00:03:02,520
is created.
38

39
00:03:02,550 --> 00:03:06,750
That is the executable. So, you can also see this exe right over here.
39

40
00:03:06,780 --> 00:03:08,200
You can execute this file.
40

41
00:03:08,280 --> 00:03:09,450
So now let's execute.
41

42
00:03:09,930 --> 00:03:15,150
So for that, what have to do is, you have to go to this path. For that, right click on this exe and select
42

43
00:03:15,150 --> 00:03:22,990
properties, and then click on this icon here. So, which will open the folder where that exe is present.
43

44
00:03:23,020 --> 00:03:25,530
So, here it is. The exe is present here.
44

45
00:03:25,600 --> 00:03:27,970
So, just launch that. As a windows
45

46
00:03:27,970 --> 00:03:29,650
command prompt application.
46

47
00:03:30,250 --> 00:03:35,040
So, that's how, you build and test the code by using this IDE for
47

48
00:03:35,050 --> 00:03:40,660
host. In the next lecture, let's learn how to create a project for the target board.

49
00:03:40,660 --> 00:03:41,620
I'll see you in the next lecture.

video 11

0
1
00:00:00,100 --> 00:00:00,410
All right. 
1

2
00:00:00,420 --> 00:00:05,940
So, now in the previous lecture you actually learnt how to create a project for the host.
2

3
00:00:06,090 --> 00:00:10,080
In this lecture, let's create a project for the target. For the target
3

4
00:00:10,080 --> 00:00:11,790
I'm not going to create here,
4

5
00:00:11,790 --> 00:00:14,660
so for that we have separate workspace.
5

6
00:00:14,700 --> 00:00:16,280
So, let's launch that.
6

7
00:00:16,290 --> 00:00:22,670
So, now I'm going to switch the workspace and here let me just type
7

8
00:00:22,690 --> 00:00:27,960
target. Let's launch. Let's create a project.
8

9
00:00:28,060 --> 00:00:36,210
Let's go to file, new, STM32 project. So, just wait until it launches
9

10
00:00:36,250 --> 00:00:37,630
the target selector.
10

11
00:00:37,690 --> 00:00:39,970
So, it is going to take some time initially,
11

12
00:00:43,420 --> 00:00:46,670
so it is initializing STM32 target selector.
12

13
00:00:46,670 --> 00:00:52,650
So, let it happen. So and it does some network activities, so let it happened.
13

14
00:00:52,660 --> 00:00:57,920
Don't cancel it.
14

15
00:00:57,950 --> 00:01:04,250
So now, it actually launches the board selector or what you can call it as target selector.
15

16
00:01:04,250 --> 00:01:06,170
Here you have to select your target.
16

17
00:01:06,290 --> 00:01:10,890
So, what kind of microcontroller you are using or what kind of board you are using?
17

18
00:01:10,910 --> 00:01:13,610
So, that's why, that is two types of selection.
18

19
00:01:13,610 --> 00:01:15,590
One is MCU selection
19

20
00:01:15,590 --> 00:01:18,510
and second one is board selection.
20

21
00:01:18,650 --> 00:01:25,370
Since, we are using a standard board which is produced by ST microelectronics that is 
21

22
00:01:25,370 --> 00:01:25,970
STM32F407
22

23
00:01:25,970 --> 00:01:28,860
discovery board, we can use board selector here.
23

24
00:01:28,880 --> 00:01:35,900
So but, if you have any custom board which is not recognized by ST, then you can just use MCU selector.
24

25
00:01:36,470 --> 00:01:42,530
Suppose, if you have any custom board based on STM32 microcontroller, then you can go for MCU selector.
25

26
00:01:43,310 --> 00:01:48,120
In all our lectures we are going to select through the board selector.
26

27
00:01:48,320 --> 00:01:55,850
So here, type your board. In our case the board is 32F4 discovery. Suppose, if you have Nucleo
27

28
00:01:55,970 --> 00:01:57,320
then you can type Nucleo.
28

29
00:01:57,710 --> 00:02:02,700
So, it will list all the Nucleo board supported select according to that.
29

30
00:02:02,720 --> 00:02:06,950
So, now let me select STM32F4  discovery.
30

31
00:02:06,950 --> 00:02:12,130
So here we can see that, it shows the picture and all the details about this board.
31

32
00:02:12,140 --> 00:02:18,260
You can browse all the details here and it also this is a product information,
32

33
00:02:18,260 --> 00:02:22,640
and if you want to get the data sheet, you can just click over here.
33

34
00:02:26,310 --> 00:02:27,780
It will just open the data sheet.
34

35
00:02:27,810 --> 00:02:36,620
So, and if you want to access all the documentation related to this board click on docs and resources.
35

36
00:02:36,700 --> 00:02:40,600
So here, you can get the data sheet and the user manual.
36

37
00:02:40,630 --> 00:02:41,950
This is required.
37

38
00:02:41,950 --> 00:02:47,980
So, you should download this user manual of the board, so all the documentation you can get right here.
38

39
00:02:48,430 --> 00:02:53,500
After that, what you have to do is, you have to click next.
39

40
00:02:53,500 --> 00:02:56,150
So, now here you have to give the project name.
40

41
00:02:56,170 --> 00:03:00,270
Let me give a project name TestProject.
41

42
00:03:00,700 --> 00:03:08,850
So here, targeted language select as C, and targeted binary type select executable, and targeted project
42

43
00:03:08,930 --> 00:03:09,530
type
43

44
00:03:09,700 --> 00:03:10,770
select empty.
44

45
00:03:10,780 --> 00:03:16,660
This is very important not STM32Cube, so do not select this unless you know how to work on
45

46
00:03:16,710 --> 00:03:18,430
STM32CubeMX software.
46

47
00:03:18,640 --> 00:03:25,870
So, after that click on finish. So, you just created the project for the target.
47

48
00:03:25,880 --> 00:03:32,690
So now you can see this source and here it has already kept our source file for you.
48

49
00:03:33,050 --> 00:03:34,720
So, that is the main.c.
49

50
00:03:34,750 --> 00:03:40,780
So here, you can start writing your application and do not delete any files from this project.
50

51
00:03:40,790 --> 00:03:44,120
So, all these files will be explained later in this course,
51

52
00:03:44,390 --> 00:03:48,440
so I am just showing in the project creation steps. After that,
52

53
00:03:48,560 --> 00:03:50,120
let's close this.
53

54
00:03:50,120 --> 00:03:54,520
After that go to console, and then try to build this project.
54

55
00:03:54,560 --> 00:03:56,950
So, let's build this project.
55

56
00:03:56,990 --> 00:04:04,220
So here it is, the cross compiler is invoked and the project is build successfully and you got the executable
56

57
00:04:04,370 --> 00:04:06,610
which is in the format .elf.
57

58
00:04:06,620 --> 00:04:12,170
So, as you make a progress in this course will also learn how to load this project into the target hardware,
58

59
00:04:12,200 --> 00:04:17,810
and how to debug the code, how to check the disassembly, how to execute the instructions
59

60
00:04:17,810 --> 00:04:22,340
one by one.So, all these things we are going to explore as we make a progress.
60

61
00:04:22,340 --> 00:04:26,770
So, with that note I would like to end this video and I'll see you in the next lecture.


━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

video 14

0
1
00:00:00,090 --> 00:00:05,710
So, in this lecture let's explore about the Embedded Target that is being used for this course.
1

2
00:00:06,300 --> 00:00:13,200
So, in this course, the STM32F407 microcontroller based DISCOVERY Board is used as
2

3
00:00:13,200 --> 00:00:15,440
the target.
3

4
00:00:15,450 --> 00:00:19,620
Now, let's explore some of the important features of this board.
4

5
00:00:19,740 --> 00:00:26,340
So, the heart of this board is actually the microcontroller, which is produced by ST microelectronics
5

6
00:00:26,340 --> 00:00:33,930
so the exact part number of the microcontroller is this one STM32F407VGT6.
6

7
00:00:33,930 --> 00:00:42,000
So, it's a 32 bit microcontroller and it is based on the processor ARM Cortex M4 with floating point
7

8
00:00:42,000 --> 00:00:43,690
unit.
8

9
00:00:43,770 --> 00:00:51,390
So, this is a microcontroller. And after that, a to connect  various external components such as LEDs,
9

10
00:00:51,420 --> 00:00:55,750
Buttons, LCDs, Keypads, and various other things.
10

11
00:00:55,800 --> 00:00:59,970
So, rich set of IO pins are available on the board.
11

12
00:00:59,970 --> 00:01:06,970
So, these are actually the IO pins or what we call as external interfacing headers (IO headers),
12

13
00:01:07,110 --> 00:01:12,680
so there are lots of IO pins are available for your external components interfacing.
13

14
00:01:12,900 --> 00:01:21,720
And this board also has 8MHz of crystal oscillator in order to supply clock to the microcontroller.
14

15
00:01:21,740 --> 00:01:23,040
Apart from that,
15

16
00:01:23,110 --> 00:01:27,200
another important feature of this board is it's onboard
16

17
00:01:27,200 --> 00:01:28,880
programmer and debugger.
17

18
00:01:28,910 --> 00:01:32,150
So, this is one of the important feature of this board.
18

19
00:01:32,210 --> 00:01:39,290
So, if you want to program and debug the code on this hardware you need not to buy any external programmer
19

20
00:01:39,350 --> 00:01:40,160
and debugger.
20

21
00:01:40,610 --> 00:01:48,140
So, the board comes with its own programmer and debugger what we call as ST-LINK/V2-A.
21

22
00:01:48,170 --> 00:01:54,920
So, by using this onboard programmer and debugger we are going to  a program and debug our code.
22

23
00:01:54,950 --> 00:02:01,580
So, when you install the IDE, that is STM32CubeIDE, the driver for this programmer and debugger
23

24
00:02:01,670 --> 00:02:04,450
will be installed automatically on your machine.
24

25
00:02:04,940 --> 00:02:11,780
Otherwise, ST also gives the separate device drivers for this ST-LINK hardware, so you can also download
25

26
00:02:11,780 --> 00:02:14,640
from the ST's website and you can install it.
26

27
00:02:14,690 --> 00:02:19,880
So, for detailed information about the board you have to refer to the user manual and schematic, which
27

28
00:02:19,880 --> 00:02:24,560
is given by the ST. So, you can go to this link and you can download a those documents,
28

29
00:02:24,560 --> 00:02:28,080
so I have given this link in the resource section of this lecture.
29

30
00:02:28,100 --> 00:02:32,360
So, when you go to that link you will find a section called resource.
30

31
00:02:32,480 --> 00:02:39,320
So, just click over that and then it will display all the related documents such as user manuals and schematics.
31

32
00:02:39,440 --> 00:02:40,690
So, just download it.
32

33
00:02:40,710 --> 00:02:45,200
So, you need not to explore these documents. So, as you make a progress in this course I will show you how 
33

34
00:02:45,200 --> 00:02:48,890
to extract information from this user manual and schematic.
34

35
00:02:48,980 --> 00:02:51,460
So, just download it and keep it in your machine.
35

36
00:02:51,530 --> 00:02:53,210
That is sufficient.
36

37
00:02:53,240 --> 00:02:55,930
So, how do you connect this board to the PC?
37

38
00:02:55,940 --> 00:03:01,720
Very very simple, you just need one mini USB cable, which is as shown in this figure.
38

39
00:03:01,760 --> 00:03:03,530
So, this cable doesn't come with the board.
39

40
00:03:03,640 --> 00:03:11,240
So, remember that, you have to procure it and then you can just use this mini USB connector of the board
40

41
00:03:11,300 --> 00:03:12,940
to connect to the PC.
41

42
00:03:12,950 --> 00:03:16,530
So, now this board also has a micro USB connector here,
42

43
00:03:16,530 --> 00:03:17,450
so don't use that.
43

44
00:03:17,460 --> 00:03:19,870
So, we don't use this in our course.
44

45
00:03:19,970 --> 00:03:24,690
So, that's the only connection you have to do in order to start using this board.
45

46
00:03:24,740 --> 00:03:26,150
So, that's about the target
46

47
00:03:26,150 --> 00:03:27,530
and I'll see you in the next lecture.


==================================================================================

section 3--your first c program

video 1

0
1
00:00:00,120 --> 00:00:06,880
All right. First let's learn some basics syntaxes related to usage of variables and some of the important
1

2
00:00:06,900 --> 00:00:12,720
standard library functions such as printf, scanf, and all. And for this I'll not be using the IDE
2

3
00:00:12,750 --> 00:00:13,690
which I installed.
3

4
00:00:13,690 --> 00:00:22,190
OK. What I do is, I use one online compiler called onlinegdb which is very much easy to use.
4

5
00:00:22,200 --> 00:00:22,560
OK
5

6
00:00:22,560 --> 00:00:28,710
and it only requires internet connection. So, you can also use this for a time being, later will use
6

7
00:00:28,710 --> 00:00:31,710
our eclipse IDE installed to write programs.
7

8
00:00:31,710 --> 00:00:32,460
OK.
8

9
00:00:32,460 --> 00:00:39,540
Of course, you cannot program your target board using this tool, but you can practice some basics syntaxes
9

10
00:00:39,600 --> 00:00:40,950
of 'C' program.
10

11
00:00:41,280 --> 00:00:48,120
But for any reason if you can't able to use this online tool, then don't worry at all. You can use the
11

12
00:00:48,120 --> 00:00:49,520
IDE install. Anyway
12

13
00:00:49,530 --> 00:00:55,700
now you already know how to create a project, compile it, and execute. Right?
13

00:00:55,740 --> 00:00:56,100
Great!

15

16

17

and other things you can change here,

Let's close this, and this is our main.c,

26

and now let's delete everything.

27
00:01:29,930 --> 00:01:34,400
So, I'm just going to press Ctrl+A and I'm going to delete everything.
27

28
00:01:34,400 --> 00:01:37,710
Let's write a simple 'C' program which displays the text
28

29
00:01:37,730 --> 00:01:38,130
Hello
29

30
00:01:38,130 --> 00:01:39,800
World on the console.
30

31
00:01:39,800 --> 00:01:40,100
All right.
31

32
00:01:40,130 --> 00:01:47,120
So, please remember that every 'C' program has at least one function that is called main function.
32

33
00:01:47,210 --> 00:01:55,190
So, main is actually the starting point of your program execution. A 'C' program is nothing but collection
33

34
00:01:55,220 --> 00:02:01,840
of various functions and it should contain at least one function and that is called as main function.
34

35
00:02:01,850 --> 00:02:06,320
So now, let's write the main function. So, don't worry about functions,
35

36
00:02:06,320 --> 00:02:08,890
it's prototype, and other details.
36

37
00:02:09,050 --> 00:02:13,700
So, we have separate section for that to understand functions. At this point
37

38
00:02:13,730 --> 00:02:20,990
it would be a too early to explore all those things. So, let's write a main function in our 'C' source file.
38

39
00:02:21,770 --> 00:02:24,980
For that, just type the function name that is main,
39

40
00:02:25,040 --> 00:02:28,720
and after that you have to put the parenthesis.
40

41
00:02:29,000 --> 00:02:30,020
So, these are the  
41

42
00:02:30,020 --> 00:02:30,630
parenthesis.
42

43
00:02:30,650 --> 00:02:38,670
And after that you had to mention the return type of this function, that is actually int
43

44
00:02:38,690 --> 00:02:40,720
this is according to the standard.
44

45
00:02:41,690 --> 00:02:49,190
So, according to the C90 and C99 standard main should return int.
45

46
00:02:49,400 --> 00:02:51,540
So, that's what standard says.
46

47
00:02:51,560 --> 00:02:51,970
All right.
47

48
00:02:51,980 --> 00:02:52,760
No problem.
48

49
00:02:52,760 --> 00:02:55,610
So, this is a prototype of the main function
49

50
00:02:55,640 --> 00:02:58,790
and this is a function name that is main.
50

51
00:02:58,820 --> 00:03:01,480
And now let's give the body of the function.
51

52
00:03:01,510 --> 00:03:07,520
So, every function will have its own body and that is identified by these brackets.
52

53
00:03:07,510 --> 00:03:07,840
All right.
53

54
00:03:07,850 --> 00:03:08,540
So now, what
54

55
00:03:08,540 --> 00:03:13,300
we just implemented is actually a function definition?
55

56
00:03:13,370 --> 00:03:15,680
So, this is called as function definition, where
56

57
00:03:15,680 --> 00:03:20,720
it has the prototype at the beginning and it has its body.
57

58
00:03:20,840 --> 00:03:28,760
So, now inside this body we are going to write code and that code will be executed by the main, when the
58

59
00:03:28,760 --> 00:03:31,160
main function is called.
59

60
00:03:31,160 --> 00:03:37,910
And in this program our job is just to print the text "Hello World" and in order to print something on
60

61
00:03:37,910 --> 00:03:46,940
the console, the 'C' standard library gives one function for us. And that function name is printf. So, printf
61

62
00:03:46,940 --> 00:03:55,250
and this is a function in order to print some text. And it can do various things but for a time
62

63
00:03:55,250 --> 00:04:03,080
being let's use this to print some text. So, write printf and after that give the parenthesis again.
63

64
00:04:03,090 --> 00:04:03,490
All right.
64

65
00:04:03,500 --> 00:04:09,220
So, and after that you have to mention your message in double quotes.
65

66
00:04:09,240 --> 00:04:11,970
So now, let's first write the double quotes.
66

67
00:04:12,060 --> 00:04:19,290
So, just write a double quote and the IDE will convert that into a two double quotes. Because, your message
67

68
00:04:19,380 --> 00:04:22,830
must be in between these double quotes.
68

69
00:04:22,830 --> 00:04:25,520
So, that's what printf expects.
69

70
00:04:25,530 --> 00:04:29,820
So, your message should be in between two double quotes.
70

71
00:04:29,820 --> 00:04:32,480
So now, here let's write our message.
71

72
00:04:32,640 --> 00:04:35,130
So, our message is Hello
72

73
00:04:35,160 --> 00:04:37,030
World.
73

74
00:04:37,070 --> 00:04:43,550
So now, in 'C' programming remember that every 'C's executable statement should end with semicolon.
74

75
00:04:44,420 --> 00:04:47,300
So, here this is the body of the function.
75

76
00:04:47,330 --> 00:04:47,690
Right?
76

77
00:04:47,750 --> 00:04:54,830
And in the body of the function we have one 'C' statement. That is actually a call to the printf function
77

78
00:04:54,890 --> 00:04:56,140
with our message.
78

79
00:04:56,180 --> 00:04:57,210
Right?
79

80
00:04:57,290 --> 00:05:01,320
And this is a 'C' statement and it should end with semicolon.
80

81
00:05:01,460 --> 00:05:04,480
So, otherwise compiler will throw errors.
81

82
00:05:05,390 --> 00:05:08,660
So, that's why, let's end this statement with a semicolon.
82

83
00:05:09,410 --> 00:05:10,480
So, this is not true
83

84
00:05:10,520 --> 00:05:16,750
if you are coming from the background of Python let's say, so in Python it is not required, but in C, C++
84

85
00:05:17,000 --> 00:05:20,660
yes, every statement should end with a semicolon.
85

86
00:05:20,660 --> 00:05:20,990
All right.
86

87
00:05:20,990 --> 00:05:28,290
So, after that, we need to do one more important thing that is we have to include a standard library header
87

88
00:05:28,310 --> 00:05:29,580
file.
88

89
00:05:29,600 --> 00:05:36,380
Now about the header file and why we should include that and all those things will understand later.
89

90
00:05:37,420 --> 00:05:42,550
So, in the later part of the course we're also going to understand about the compilation process and
90

91
00:05:42,640 --> 00:05:44,460
various other things.
91

92
00:05:44,680 --> 00:05:46,370
So, let's not worry about that.
92

93
00:05:46,510 --> 00:05:53,680
But, remember that in this source file we have used a function called printf which is not written by
93

94
00:05:53,680 --> 00:05:54,370
us,
94

95
00:05:54,370 --> 00:06:03,760
it is given by the standard library of 'C'. And compiler should need to understand the prototype of
95

96
00:06:03,760 --> 00:06:13,630
that printf function. And the prototype of that printf function is written in a file called header file and
96

97
00:06:13,630 --> 00:06:17,670
that header file name is stdio.h,
97

98
00:06:17,770 --> 00:06:26,350
and we have to include that file into our project. And now to include a file you have to use #include
98

99
00:06:26,530 --> 00:06:31,090
directive. So now, it is written something like this #include.
99

100
00:06:31,090 --> 00:06:41,920
So, this is a directive. Which is used to indicate inclusion of another file and that another file name
100

101
00:06:41,920 --> 00:06:42,720
is  
101

102
00:06:42,760 --> 00:06:44,610
stdio.h.
102

103
00:06:44,740 --> 00:06:51,610
This is written by the technical committee or the developers who develop the 'C' standard library.
103

104
00:06:51,700 --> 00:06:58,570
So now, since it's the standard library header file it has to be enclosed by these symbols.
104

105
00:06:59,020 --> 00:07:00,010
So, this is a way
105

106
00:07:00,040 --> 00:07:03,840
you include stdio.h.
106

107
00:07:03,850 --> 00:07:08,410
So, this is a include directive and this is a file name,
107

108
00:07:08,410 --> 00:07:15,470
and since it is from standard library, so you have to use this a lesser than and greater than symbols. It
108

109
00:07:15,490 --> 00:07:16,050
is fine.
109

110
00:07:16,720 --> 00:07:20,680
So, you can also include a header file which is written by you.
110

111
00:07:20,680 --> 00:07:24,190
So, that is also possible and more on that will see later.
111

112
00:07:24,190 --> 00:07:24,480
All right.
112

113
00:07:24,490 --> 00:07:30,130
Now let's compile and run our code. And for that, in this IDE there is only one button which will compile
113

114
00:07:30,130 --> 00:07:33,980
and run your code and you have to press this run button.
114

115
00:07:34,180 --> 00:07:40,210
Let's click over here. And here you can see that, it is compiling and the output is produced and it is
115

116
00:07:40,210 --> 00:07:43,840
successfully printed the text "Hello World" on the console.
116

117
00:07:44,440 --> 00:07:48,480
So, you successfully completed the very first 'C' application.
117

118
00:07:48,490 --> 00:07:48,790
All right.
118

119
00:07:48,790 --> 00:07:55,960
So, now I want you to do one small settings in this project. So, go to the settings here, and go to the
119

120
00:07:55,960 --> 00:07:57,240
compiler flags,
120

121
00:07:57,310 --> 00:08:01,140
and here just give hyphen and type save temps.
121

122
00:08:01,150 --> 00:08:09,400
So, just write this compiler argument. So, this is a compiler argument save temps, and click OK, and click done
122

123
00:08:09,400 --> 00:08:12,260
here, and after that again run the file.
123

124
00:08:12,370 --> 00:08:13,360
And here it is.
124

125
00:08:13,360 --> 00:08:15,920
So, it has created so many files.
125

126
00:08:16,090 --> 00:08:21,420
So, these are the output files produced in the process of compilation.
126

127
00:08:21,460 --> 00:08:27,070
Remember that, this is a main.c written by you, which is a source file which is given to the compiler
127

128
00:08:27,070 --> 00:08:28,580
for compilation.
128

129
00:08:28,930 --> 00:08:37,810
And the first file which is produced during the compilation is a pre processed file. So, that we call as
129

130
00:08:37,810 --> 00:08:39,520
main.i.
130

131
00:08:39,520 --> 00:08:45,850
And here you can see that, main.i is nothing but the collection of your code
131

132
00:08:45,850 --> 00:08:54,230
this is written by you and contents of the stdio.h. Now, you are main.c converted into  
132

133
00:08:54,250 --> 00:08:54,630
main.i
133

134
00:08:54,660 --> 00:08:56,670
which is a huge file. Isn't it?
134

135
00:08:56,700 --> 00:08:59,400
So, your file had only 8 lines.
135

136
00:08:59,400 --> 00:09:00,950
But look at main.i
136

137
00:09:00,960 --> 00:09:04,500
it now has 849 lines.
137

138
00:09:04,500 --> 00:09:06,620
So, what happened was here?
138

139
00:09:06,690 --> 00:09:11,710
So, when compiler saw this line that is stdio.h,
139

140
00:09:11,880 --> 00:09:20,520
compiler included all the contents of this file into this main.c and then it converted that file
140

141
00:09:20,520 --> 00:09:28,050
to main.i, which is a output of the pre processor stage of the compilation process.
141

142
00:09:30,890 --> 00:09:40,040
And here, the compiler actually looks at the prototype of this function. So, which is written somewhere
142

143
00:09:40,040 --> 00:09:40,670
here.
143

144
00:09:40,700 --> 00:09:42,260
You have to search that.
144

145
00:09:42,500 --> 00:09:44,930
So here, that is no search option actually,
145

146
00:09:44,960 --> 00:09:52,050
so what I would do is, I would just copy this contents and here I will just search for printf.
146

147
00:09:52,190 --> 00:09:53,600
So, which  is right here.
147

148
00:09:53,600 --> 00:09:57,950
Just search for printf and just click on match whole word only.
148

149
00:09:58,010 --> 00:09:58,700
And here it is,
149

150
00:09:58,700 --> 00:10:01,880
this is a printf function called by you.
150

151
00:10:01,880 --> 00:10:02,870
And here it is.
151

152
00:10:02,900 --> 00:10:07,430
So, this is a prototype of the function printf.
152

153
00:10:07,890 --> 00:10:12,830
So, compiler want this signature, so this is also called as a function signature.
153

154
00:10:13,370 --> 00:10:20,140
So, that's a purpose why we added that stdio.h. So, first main.i is produced,
154

155
00:10:20,280 --> 00:10:27,880
then after that main.i is converted into main.s which is collection of assembly instructions.
155

156
00:10:28,050 --> 00:10:34,740
So, your higher level language is converted into a little lower level language that is called as assembly
156

157
00:10:35,010 --> 00:10:42,600
language. So, now assembly instructions are generated for your .c file. And after that this
157

158
00:10:42,600 --> 00:10:48,430
will be converted into a machine code and that we call as main.o.
158

159
00:10:49,020 --> 00:10:56,550
So, this is also called as object file. So, you can see that it is not possible to read the contents of
159

160
00:10:56,640 --> 00:11:00,370
this file, because it contains the machine code.
160

161
00:11:00,570 --> 00:11:06,930
So, more on that will see later again and we will discuss all these things when we actually do programming
161

162
00:11:06,930 --> 00:11:08,400
on our target.
162

163
00:11:08,400 --> 00:11:11,520
So, that is our Embedded Target. At this point
163

164
00:11:11,520 --> 00:11:17,880
I just wanted to briefly introduce the intermediate files. So, all these intermediate files you can get
164

165
00:11:17,940 --> 00:11:23,400
by adding the compiler flag called save temps and I'm going to remove that.
165

166
00:11:23,430 --> 00:11:30,070
So, it is not required for each and every project. So, that's about the very first application
166

167
00:11:30,070 --> 00:11:34,020
Hello World and I will give you a small assignment.
167

168
00:11:34,020 --> 00:11:34,330
All right.
168

169
00:11:34,330 --> 00:11:36,670
So now I will give you one assignment.
169

170
00:11:36,670 --> 00:11:42,340
So, you have to modify the program what we have just returned to print these messages.
170

171
00:11:42,340 --> 00:11:46,380
So, in the first line it has to print "Hello World" and in the second line it has to print
171

172
00:11:46,420 --> 00:11:47,690
"Today is a great day".
172

173
00:11:47,710 --> 00:11:52,950
So, just try to think how you can able to achieve this and you can use two printf statements,
173

174
00:11:52,960 --> 00:11:54,400
no problem.
174

175
00:11:54,430 --> 00:11:57,810
So, just think how you can do this and I'll see you in the next lecture.

video 3

0
1
00:00:00,080 --> 00:00:01,230
Hey, welcome back to the lecture.
1

2
00:00:01,230 --> 00:00:05,270
So, in the previous lecture I actually gave you one small assignment. Right?
2

3
00:00:05,310 --> 00:00:06,630
So, if you couldn't able to do that,
3

4
00:00:06,630 --> 00:00:07,410
no problem.
4

5
00:00:07,820 --> 00:00:13,320
Let's try in this lecture. So, our requirement was to print this text
5

6
00:00:13,320 --> 00:00:16,440
that is "Today is a great day" in a new line. Isn't it?
6

7
00:00:16,620 --> 00:00:17,700
Let's see how you can do that.
7

8
00:00:17,730 --> 00:00:18,030
All right.
8

9
00:00:18,030 --> 00:00:22,830
Most of you might have tried like this and now let's see how this goes.
9

10
00:00:23,070 --> 00:00:23,760
Let's run.
10

11
00:00:24,480 --> 00:00:25,740
And here we can see that,
11

12
00:00:25,770 --> 00:00:30,530
so this is not actually our expected output. Right?
12

13
00:00:30,530 --> 00:00:33,780
So here we want to push this text to the next line.
13

14
00:00:34,500 --> 00:00:38,890
So, by this you can understand the behavior of the printf function.
14

15
00:00:39,120 --> 00:00:44,740
So, printf actually never causes cursor to move to the next line.
15

16
00:00:44,850 --> 00:00:48,240
So, unless you mention the printf to do that.
16

17
00:00:48,240 --> 00:00:51,520
So, let's give couple of space here in order to comprehend this.
17

18
00:00:51,540 --> 00:00:54,990
So, let me give couple of space here and let me run.
18

19
00:00:54,990 --> 00:01:00,810
And here you can see that, when the first printf is executed the cursor was here.
19

20
00:01:00,990 --> 00:01:01,580
Right?
20

21
00:01:01,590 --> 00:01:07,860
And after that, when the next printf is executed the printing is actually continued from there onwards.
21

22
00:01:07,860 --> 00:01:08,970
Right?
22

23
00:01:09,000 --> 00:01:13,590
So, that's the behavior a which you can notice from this code. 
23

24
00:01:13,720 --> 00:01:14,970
Right?
24

25
00:01:14,990 --> 00:01:24,170
So now, how to tell the printf to push the cursor to the next line.So, that you can tell by passing
25

26
00:01:24,200 --> 00:01:32,730
some code to the printf function. And that code is n, n means new line or line feed.
26

27
00:01:32,730 --> 00:01:35,690
So, but you cannot simply write n here.
27

28
00:01:35,690 --> 00:01:36,140
Right?
28

29
00:01:36,260 --> 00:01:37,990
So, what happens if you simply write 
29

30
00:01:38,000 --> 00:01:41,720
n?  So, it will be treated just like another character.
30

31
00:01:41,720 --> 00:01:42,410
Right?
31

32
00:01:42,410 --> 00:01:43,210
If I run this
32

33
00:01:43,220 --> 00:01:44,760
what happens, n will be printed now.
33

34
00:01:44,790 --> 00:01:45,500
right?
34

35
00:01:45,530 --> 00:01:47,570
So, that is actually expected output.
35

36
00:01:48,440 --> 00:01:52,930
So, in order to give special meaning to this character,
36

37
00:01:52,930 --> 00:02:01,410
so you have to use an escape character. And in 'C' the escape character is backslash (\).
37

38
00:02:01,460 --> 00:02:06,770
Let's give a backslash character.Write slash backslash.
38

39
00:02:06,770 --> 00:02:17,120
So, now this is called as an escape sequence. An escape sequence is collection of two characters. One is
39

40
00:02:17,240 --> 00:02:21,130
backslash and another one is code.
40

41
00:02:21,170 --> 00:02:26,150
So, here n is a code to push the cursor to the new line.
41

42
00:02:26,150 --> 00:02:31,500
So, you can use this escape sequence wherever you want, it need not be at the end.
42

43
00:02:31,730 --> 00:02:33,560
You can use it anywhere you want.
43

44
00:02:33,590 --> 00:02:36,020
Let's compile the code once again.
44

45
00:02:36,020 --> 00:02:36,800
And here it is.
45

46
00:02:36,830 --> 00:02:39,140
So, we now got the expected output.
46

47
00:02:39,140 --> 00:02:39,770
So, now here
47

48
00:02:39,770 --> 00:02:41,270
what happened was,
48

49
00:02:41,270 --> 00:02:48,950
so when printf printed all these characters it actually saw a special sequence here.
49

50
00:02:48,980 --> 00:02:56,810
So, that means it will treat the character n as a code to push the cursor to the second line or new
50

51
00:02:56,810 --> 00:03:05,680
line. So, like this in 'C' there are various escape sequences. So, you can give this escape sequence anywhere
51

52
00:03:05,680 --> 00:03:06,100
you want,
52

53
00:03:06,100 --> 00:03:12,160
so I can give here, I can give here, I can give here, here.
53

54
00:03:12,400 --> 00:03:14,640
Let's see what is the output of this program,
54

55
00:03:14,650 --> 00:03:16,920
and here you can see that this is a output.
55

56
00:03:17,890 --> 00:03:19,030
So, after W
56

57
00:03:19,120 --> 00:03:25,930
so this is treated as an escape sequence to push the cursor to the new line.
57

58
00:03:25,990 --> 00:03:28,940
So, that's why, cursor went to the new line
58

59
00:03:29,020 --> 00:03:34,140
and after that it printed o, and again cursor went to the new line
59

60
00:03:34,150 --> 00:03:36,670
it printed r, and so on.
60

61
00:03:36,670 --> 00:03:37,020
All right.
61

62
00:03:37,030 --> 00:03:43,250
So, in the next lecture let's discuss some more escape sequences available in 'C' programming language.
62

63
00:03:43,270 --> 00:03:44,640
I'll see you in the next lecture.


━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

video 4

0
1
00:00:00,090 --> 00:00:05,100
Hey, welcome back to the lecture. So, in this lecture let's explore about escape sequences which are available
1

2
00:00:05,100 --> 00:00:11,460
in 'C' programming language. And in the previous lecture we actually explored about one escape sequence
2

3
00:00:11,490 --> 00:00:15,330
that is \n, which is nothing but new line
3

4
00:00:15,330 --> 00:00:21,650
or line feed. And apart from that there are various other escape sequences are available which you can
4

5
00:00:21,680 --> 00:00:29,030
use in your program along with the printf function and you can also use this with other standard library
5

6
00:00:29,030 --> 00:00:30,090
functions.
6

7
00:00:30,260 --> 00:00:37,460
For example, \t is used for inserting horizontal tab. So, a tab is nothing but a collection of
7

8
00:00:37,520 --> 00:00:38,740
four spaces.
8

9
00:00:39,410 --> 00:00:48,070
So, you can use \t in order to insert a tab and also a \0 is considered as a null character.
9

10
00:00:48,140 --> 00:00:56,200
So, more on that we'll see later when we talk about strings and after that \r is known as a carriage return,
10

11
00:00:56,300 --> 00:00:58,190
and about that we'll discuss in a moment.
11

12
00:00:58,190 --> 00:01:04,800
Don't worry. And if you want to print backslash, then you can use this escape sequence
12

13
00:01:04,880 --> 00:01:11,000
and if you want to print double quote, then you can use this escape sequence.
13

14
00:01:11,000 --> 00:01:16,100
So now let's understand what is the difference between \n and \r.
14

15
00:01:16,260 --> 00:01:21,920
So, remember that, \n moves the cursor to the beginning of the new line
15

16
00:01:21,920 --> 00:01:29,790
vertically. But, \r moves the cursor to the beginning of the current line horizontally.
16

17
00:01:29,790 --> 00:01:30,890
Now to understand this
17

18
00:01:30,890 --> 00:01:32,750
let's do a small exercise.
18

19
00:01:32,810 --> 00:01:36,080
So now here, let's modify this application.
19

20
00:01:36,470 --> 00:01:43,150
So, now here I'm going to print "Hello World" and I'm going to give \r instead of \n. And here
20

21
00:01:43,180 --> 00:01:47,390
I'm going to remove this text and I'm just going to write "bye".
21

22
00:01:47,500 --> 00:01:49,630
Let's see how the output looks like.
22

23
00:01:49,630 --> 00:01:56,960
Let's run this code and here it is. So, the output you can see that it is just printing "bye ! Word !"
23

24
00:01:57,680 --> 00:02:00,370
So, now let's analyze what's happening here.
24

25
00:02:00,370 --> 00:02:07,240
So, remember that, \r moves the cursor to the beginning of the current line horizontally.
25

26
00:02:07,450 --> 00:02:09,290
So to understand this.
26

27
00:02:09,340 --> 00:02:15,430
So, this is a program we have written and when we execute first printf,
27

28
00:02:15,550 --> 00:02:18,540
so this is what happens.
28

29
00:02:18,790 --> 00:02:25,180
So, the output will be Hello World and cursor will be here, at the last character.
29

30
00:02:25,180 --> 00:02:25,480
Right?
30

31
00:02:26,050 --> 00:02:27,610
And this is line 1.
31

32
00:02:27,850 --> 00:02:28,780
So, which is this one.
32

33
00:02:28,780 --> 00:02:29,700
This is a line 1
33

34
00:02:29,710 --> 00:02:32,070
let's say. So, "Hello World" will be printed.
34

35
00:02:32,890 --> 00:02:37,210
And after that, there is a \r. Isn't it?
35

36
00:02:37,210 --> 00:02:40,590
So, when printf encounters \r,
36

37
00:02:40,660 --> 00:02:47,910
then what happens is this cursor will be moved to the beginning of the current line horizontally.
37

38
00:02:48,550 --> 00:02:55,900
So, the next printf will execute now which is from here, because this is now the current position of
38

39
00:02:55,900 --> 00:02:56,440
the cursor.
39

40
00:02:57,040 --> 00:03:01,600
So, that's why, the old text will be overwritten with a new string
40

41
00:03:01,600 --> 00:03:02,610
that is "bye".
41

42
00:03:03,340 --> 00:03:07,270
And now this is a position of the new cursor.
42

43
00:03:08,320 --> 00:03:11,550
So, that's the reason why you see this output.
43

44
00:03:11,890 --> 00:03:16,330
Alright. So, with that note I would like to end this video, but before ending this video I would like to
44

45
00:03:16,330 --> 00:03:18,730
give you one small assignment.
45

46
00:03:18,910 --> 00:03:24,480
So, write a program which displays these two strings.
46

47
00:03:24,500 --> 00:03:26,850
The first line should look something like this
47

48
00:03:26,850 --> 00:03:29,800
David says, "programming is fun!".
48

49
00:03:29,840 --> 00:03:38,060
Remember that, you should display this text inside the double quotations and also in the second line
49

50
00:03:38,090 --> 00:03:39,100
you should print
50

51
00:03:39,140 --> 00:03:46,890
"Good day!". So, try to modify a printf statements to print something like this.
51

52
00:03:46,920 --> 00:03:48,230
I'll see you in the next lecture.

0
1
00:00:00,080 --> 00:00:01,170
Hey, welcome back to the lecture.

2
00:00:01,170 --> 00:00:07,010
So, in the previous lecture I actually explained about the escape sequences and also I gave one assignment. 
2

3
00:00:07,020 --> 00:00:11,960
I hope you completed that and if you couldn't able to a complete that assignment,
3

4
00:00:11,970 --> 00:00:12,930
so now let's try
4

5
00:00:12,930 --> 00:00:13,880
in this lecture.
