2

3
00:00:06,130 --> 00:00:11,340
Let's try to solve this issue. To solve this issue we have to understand the return value of scanf.
3

4
00:00:12,060 --> 00:00:17,730
So, we had something like this scanf %f and num1 to read a number.
4

5
00:00:17,940 --> 00:00:18,750
Strictly speaking
5

6
00:00:18,750 --> 00:00:20,530
to read a real number.
6

7
00:00:20,910 --> 00:00:23,760
So, you should always remember that scanf returns
7

8
00:00:23,790 --> 00:00:29,040
total number of inputs scanned successfully from the standard input.
8

9
00:00:29,070 --> 00:00:33,320
So, in this above case scanf actually returns 1,
9

10
00:00:33,420 --> 00:00:35,730
if the scan is successful. Why?
10

11
00:00:35,760 --> 00:00:38,570
Because, there is the 1 item to be scanned,
11

12
00:00:38,590 --> 00:00:41,590
so that is of type %f, that is a real number.
12

13
00:00:42,120 --> 00:00:44,990
So, for this case a scanf returns 1,
13

14
00:00:45,000 --> 00:00:50,460
if the scan is successful. If the scan is unsuccessful then scanf returns 0.
14

15
00:00:50,460 --> 00:00:58,020
So, when you enter a character for the above code, the scanf fails and returns 0 indicating
15

16
00:00:58,170 --> 00:01:05,540
it has reached end of file of the input stream. So, 0 just indicates that scan is failed.
16

17
00:01:05,610 --> 00:01:12,300
So, the scan fails for the character input because it was expecting a number, a scanf was expecting
17

18
00:01:12,300 --> 00:01:12,720
a number.
18

19
00:01:12,750 --> 00:01:15,390
So, you cannot type a character.
19

20
00:01:15,390 --> 00:01:23,220
So, when you type a character the scan actually fails and scanf returns immediately with a value 0.
20

21
00:01:23,270 --> 00:01:27,690
So, based on the return value of the scanf, now we can modify our code.
21

22
00:01:27,800 --> 00:01:35,900
So, if scanf return 0, then conclude that user has entered something else, it's a invalid input and
22

23
00:01:35,930 --> 00:01:37,970
based on that you can take some action.
23

24
00:01:38,060 --> 00:01:42,810
So, let's modify the code by checking the return value of the scanf.
24

25
00:01:42,890 --> 00:01:46,590
Now I'm in the IDE and let's modify this code.
25

26

So, what I want to do is, I would like to check the return value of the scanf.
26

27
00:01:51,590 --> 00:01:59,200
So, here let's use one more if statement for the scanf. So, let's use scanf itself as an expression,
27

28
00:01:59,240 --> 00:02:00,550
you can use that.
28

29
00:02:00,590 --> 00:02:07,490
So, just remove this semicolon (;) which was there with the scanf. So because, now you are using a scanf as
29

30
00:02:07,490 --> 00:02:08,790
an expression.
30

31
00:02:08,870 --> 00:02:14,660
So, keep scanf inside the parentheses of the if. So, when this is executed,
31

32
00:02:14,660 --> 00:02:18,020
so in this case this expression returns 1,
32

33
00:02:18,050 --> 00:02:19,690
if the scan is successful.
33

34
00:02:19,850 --> 00:02:23,900
So, this function call returns 0, if the scan is unsuccessful.
34

35
00:02:23,960 --> 00:02:27,760
So, let's compare the return value. If this is equal to 0
35

36
00:02:27,770 --> 00:02:30,180
that means that scan has failed.
36

37
00:02:30,200 --> 00:02:31,760
So here, we are just checking,
37

38
00:02:31,790 --> 00:02:35,690
so here we are comparing the return value of the scanf with 0.
38

39
00:02:35,690 --> 00:02:43,610
So, if this is true, then you can print "Invalid input" and you can type and you can exit the app exiting,
39

40
00:02:44,000 --> 00:02:47,540
and you can return from the application here itself.
40

41
00:02:47,540 --> 00:02:48,440
So, don't proceed.
41

42
00:02:48,440 --> 00:02:49,370
Just return.
42

43
00:02:49,370 --> 00:02:56,040
So, you can even pass some negative numbers in order to indicate it's a failure, but you can just use
43

44
00:02:56,090 --> 00:02:57,870
return 0 for this application,
44

45
00:02:57,890 --> 00:02:59,060
it doesn't matter actually.
45

46
00:02:59,090 --> 00:03:00,870
So, let's just use return 0.
46

47
00:03:00,920 --> 00:03:03,790
Let's do the same thing for this scanf as
47

48
00:03:03,800 --> 00:03:08,460
well. Let me just copy the same code and paste here make this as num2.
48

49
00:03:08,510 --> 00:03:10,080
You can do that. Here,
49

50

you are actually not waiting for the user input, so it is returning immediately.

51
00:03:15,170 --> 00:03:21,370
So, you may have to introduce this code. So, I hope you are understanding this code

52
00:03:21,420 --> 00:03:32,090
and so let's do one thing. So, let's create a small function void wait_for_user_input. It's a void function, now

00:03:32,130 --> 00:03:33,610
let's keep this code,

here at the top,

and after that call this function from wherever you want.

and here as well.

we are actually just checking the return value of the scanf and comparing that with 0.

so let's close this. And let's first let's try with our numbers,

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

video 8

0
1
00:00:00,090 --> 00:00:04,170
So, in this lecture let's explore about if-else-if ladder.
1

2
00:00:04,230 --> 00:00:08,610
So, here is a syntax of if-else-if ladder statement.
2

3
00:00:08,610 --> 00:00:11,250
This is similar to if-else statement,
3

4
00:00:11,310 --> 00:00:18,610
but the difference between if-else and if-else-if ladder is, so in the case of if-else statement
4

5
00:00:18,630 --> 00:00:21,000
so you have only one expression,
5

6
00:00:21,000 --> 00:00:23,040
so based on that decision is taken.
6

7
00:00:23,370 --> 00:00:31,910
But in the case of if-else-if ladder, so you can evaluate multiple expressions. So, observe this syntax
7

8
00:00:31,920 --> 00:00:33,850
so here there is if block.
8

9
00:00:33,900 --> 00:00:39,300
So, this is a if block, and you can use else if block along with that.
9

10
00:00:39,690 --> 00:00:45,590
So, you can have N number of else if blocks. And at the end you can have else block. 
10

11
00:00:45,630 --> 00:00:47,320
So, this is actually optional.
11

12
00:00:47,490 --> 00:00:49,290
So, let's see how this works.
12

13
00:00:49,350 --> 00:00:53,880
So, first the expression of the if block will be evaluated.
13

14
00:00:54,120 --> 00:01:02,250
If this is true, then the statements of the if block will be executed and all the other blocks will be
14

15
00:01:02,250 --> 00:01:06,210
skipped.Suppose, if this expression fails
15

16
00:01:06,210 --> 00:01:11,450
if it is false, then only the next expression will be evaluated.
16

17
00:01:11,460 --> 00:01:19,560
So, in this case expression2 will be evaluated only if expression1 is false. So, if expression2 is let's
17

18
00:01:19,560 --> 00:01:24,070
say false, then expression3 will be evaluated like that.
18

19
00:01:24,100 --> 00:01:31,620
So, if none of the expressions are true, then the default block that is else will be executed.
19

20
00:01:31,680 --> 00:01:38,490
So, the statements of the else block will be executed only if all the above expressions are false.
20

21
00:01:38,490 --> 00:01:47,250
So, here is a flowchart of if-else-if ladder, so first if expression will be evaluated. If it is true, then
21

22
00:01:47,250 --> 00:01:56,220
statements of the if block will be executed and all the other else if blocks will be skipped and the program
22

23
00:01:56,250 --> 00:01:58,210
continues with the rest of the code.
23

24
00:01:58,230 --> 00:02:03,330
So, if this expression fails, then only expression2 will be evaluated.
24

25
00:02:03,420 --> 00:02:04,500
So, that's all about
25

26
00:02:04,500 --> 00:02:10,440
if-else-if ladder and in the next lecture let's do one exercise using if-else-if ladder and then we can
26

27
00:02:10,440 --> 00:02:13,040
explore about the conditional operators.
27

28
00:02:13,080 --> 00:02:13,980
I'll see you in the next lecture.


━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

1
00:00:00,060 --> 00:00:00,340
All right.

2
00:00:00,360 --> 00:00:02,100
So, now let's do one exercise.
2

3
00:00:02,100 --> 00:00:03,890
The exercise is very simple.
3

4
00:00:03,900 --> 00:00:08,360
Write a program to calculate income tax payable of the user.
4

5
00:00:08,370 --> 00:00:11,420
Now the tax is calculated as per the below table.
5

6
00:00:12,150 --> 00:00:18,060
So, this is a formula to calculate the tax. So, you have to multiply income by the tax rate.
6

7
00:00:18,060 --> 00:00:19,570
So, these are the tax rate.
7

8
00:00:19,800 --> 00:00:25,770
So, for this application your program has to first ask the user about the total income.
8

9
00:00:26,190 --> 00:00:28,870
So, you have to get the total income by the user,
9

10
00:00:28,890 --> 00:00:35,310
so then multiply with the appropriate tax rate to the income to calculate the tax payable, and then you can
10

11
00:00:35,310 --> 00:00:36,780
print the tax.
11

12
00:00:36,780 --> 00:00:41,640
So, I want you to code this exercise using if-else-if statements, 
12

13
00:00:41,640 --> 00:00:48,790
and this is how the output looks like. So, first it should ask the user to enter the total income, so
13

14
00:00:48,810 --> 00:00:49,680
let's say I type
14

15
00:00:49,680 --> 00:00:50,790
1000 dollars.
15

16
00:00:50,790 --> 00:00:54,450
Just type 1000 and it should give me the tax payable.
16

17
00:00:54,660 --> 00:00:59,880
So, in this case it is 0, or let's say if I type 100k
17

18
00:01:00,060 --> 00:01:05,730
so it should calculate and it should give the tax payable. So, try to do this exercise
18

19
00:01:05,760 --> 00:01:08,360
and I'll see you in the next lecture. So, in the next lecture
19

20
00:01:08,370 --> 00:01:09,890
I will code for this exercise.

1
00:00:00,150 --> 00:00:05,370
Hey, welcome back to the lecture. So, let's code this exercise tax calculating exercise.
1

2
00:00:05,520 --> 00:00:07,920
First of all a let's create some variables.
2

3
00:00:08,070 --> 00:00:10,350
So, let's just stick to integers.
3

4
00:00:10,350 --> 00:00:15,810
So, I would use uint64_t to hold the income.
4

5
00:00:15,810 --> 00:00:21,160
So, I think 64 bit width is sufficient to hold billions of dollars.
5

6
00:00:21,330 --> 00:00:25,560
So, that's why, I would just stick to income as 64 bit value.
6

7
00:00:25,920 --> 00:00:34,920
And after that, again let's consider another variable of 64_t and this variable is tax to store the tax
7

8
00:00:34,920 --> 00:00:36,180
calculated.
8

9
00:00:36,420 --> 00:00:40,640
First let's ask the user to enter the income. So here,
9

10
00:00:40,700 --> 00:00:41,710
what I would do is,
10

11
00:00:41,720 --> 00:00:46,990
so now I will just create one temporary variable here, double is equal to let's say temp,
11

12
00:00:47,510 --> 00:00:48,670
temporary income.
12

13
00:00:48,890 --> 00:00:53,720
So, I would use a double or float to create a variable a temporary variable.
13

14
00:00:54,050 --> 00:01:02,020
So, after that, I will read the user input into the temporary income variable. So, scanf for double
14

15
00:01:02,020 --> 00:01:07,630
let's use %lf, &temp_income.
15

16
00:01:07,700 --> 00:01:12,140
And after that, I would store this temporary income into the income.
16

17
00:01:12,320 --> 00:01:14,940
So, I am just storing the integer part.
17

18
00:01:14,960 --> 00:01:16,730
This is of type uint_64,
18

19
00:01:16,790 --> 00:01:18,300
so this is type double.
19

20
00:01:18,320 --> 00:01:20,720
So, the compiler may issues some warning here.
20

21
00:01:20,780 --> 00:01:29,050
So, that's why, it's better to typecast this into 64_t. So, this is done just to extract the
21

22
00:01:29,050 --> 00:01:34,170
integer part. Because, user may enter a real number and he can break the application.
22

23
00:01:34,200 --> 00:01:40,660
So, that's why, I'm just taking the input as a real number and after that just taking the integer part.
23

24
00:01:41,290 --> 00:01:43,060
So, let's start comparing.
24

25
00:01:43,060 --> 00:01:51,430
So, if(income <= 9525),  then tax is 0. Tax = 0.

26
00:01:51,970 --> 00:01:58,270
So now, how do you do the next comparison? So, do you use else or do you use something different.
26

27
00:01:58,270 --> 00:02:04,540
So, if you use else you can do that, you can use else no problem.If you use else again you have to use if condition
27

28
00:02:04,540 --> 00:02:11,350
here for the further comparison. So, we can solve this exercise by using if-else-if ladder.
28

29
00:02:11,380 --> 00:02:17,830
So, instead of using else here, what you can do is, you can use another expression, so you can evaluate
29

30
00:02:17,860 --> 00:02:23,220
another expression by using else if. So, just type else give a space
30

31
00:02:23,230 --> 00:02:24,350
space is important,
31

32
00:02:24,370 --> 00:02:26,180
otherwise it will not recognize that.
32

33
00:02:26,620 --> 00:02:34,180
So, else space if else if, open the parentheses for another expression and after that let's open the body
33

34
00:02:34,240 --> 00:02:35,940
of the else if block.
34

35
00:02:36,010 --> 00:02:42,010
So, now here you have to mention another expression. Another expression depends upon our work.
35

36
00:02:42,040 --> 00:02:43,690
So, we have to evaluate
36

37
00:02:43,690 --> 00:02:47,290
we have to compare the income between these boundary values.
37

38
00:02:47,290 --> 00:02:48,090
How do you do that?
38

39
00:02:48,130 --> 00:02:52,690
So, for this we have to use relational operators like lesser than, greater than.
39

40
00:02:52,720 --> 00:02:58,480
So, what I would do is, if income is greater than this value,
40

41
00:02:59,200 --> 00:03:10,570
so this is let's keep this in a parentheses and logical-AND income lesser than or is equal to this value
41

42
00:03:10,610 --> 00:03:12,230
38,700. 
42

43
00:03:12,250 --> 00:03:14,180
So, these are the boundary values.
43

44
00:03:14,230 --> 00:03:21,310
So, in this case tax = income * 0.12.
44

45
00:03:21,340 --> 00:03:23,280
So, if you didn't get this expression,
45

46
00:03:23,290 --> 00:03:25,690
so let me explain you with PPT.

47
00:03:25,720 --> 00:03:26,630
So, here it is.
47

48
00:03:26,650 --> 00:03:28,030
This is what we did.
48

49
00:03:28,060 --> 00:03:31,240
This is actually one else if block.
49

50

So, this is a parentheses of the else if and this is a compound expression,
50

51
00:03:36,880 --> 00:03:39,720
and this is actually a logical expression.
51

52
00:03:39,730 --> 00:03:40,180
Why?
52

53
00:03:40,180 --> 00:03:45,390
Because, here is a logical-AND and this is a sub expression

54

let me call expression1 and this is another sub expression

55
00:03:49,030 --> 00:03:50,450
let me call 2.

56
00:03:50,500 --> 00:03:55,850
So, this is a operand1 and this is a operand2 of the logical-AND operator.
56

57
00:03:56,380 --> 00:03:59,590
So, first this sub expression will be evaluated.
57

58
00:03:59,950 --> 00:04:03,250
So, because that is inside the parentheses.
58

59
00:04:03,250 --> 00:04:06,300
So, if you start evaluating from left to right.
59

60
00:04:06,370 --> 00:04:09,110
So, let's say, this evaluation turns out to be true,
60

61
00:04:09,190 --> 00:04:12,100
and after that this expression will be evaluated.
61

62
00:04:12,220 --> 00:04:14,100
So, this is actually operand2.
62

63
00:04:14,170 --> 00:04:16,900
So, let's say this also turns out to be true.
63

64
00:04:17,440 --> 00:04:23,850
So, true logical-AND with true, that means the whole expression turns out to be true.
64

65
00:04:23,860 --> 00:04:27,340
So, that's why, in that case this statement will be executed.
65

66
00:04:28,030 --> 00:04:34,590
So, if either one of these expression turns out to be false, then this statement will not be executed.
66

67
00:04:34,600 --> 00:04:36,190
It's like that.
67

68
00:04:36,190 --> 00:04:42,210
So here, these are the boundary values we are actually checking against the income using relational operators
68

69
00:04:42,250 --> 00:04:49,690
and after that we are using logical-AND to decide whether the income really falls in between these a boundary
69

70
00:04:49,690 --> 00:04:50,690
values.
70

71
00:04:50,740 --> 00:04:58,810
So similarly, we can use one more else if for our next comparison, just copy this and paste here.

72
00:04:58,840 --> 00:05:05,140
So, in this case, if income is greater than this value and lesser than or is equal to this value  
72

73

82,500.

74

75

And again you have to use one more  else if, and in this case this is a very simple expression.

76

If income is greater than this value, then this value will be 32 and tax = tax + 1000

78

79
00:05:38,320 --> 00:05:40,410
So, here there is some problem,

so this should come here.

81
00:05:41,770 --> 00:05:50,170
And after that let's print the tax,  tax payable %I64u,

82
00:05:50,210 --> 00:05:55,130
I would use u, because all tax and income calculations are always positive.

84

86

87
00:06:08,640 --> 00:06:08,970
All right.

88
00:06:08,970 --> 00:06:12,030
So, just take a look into this multiplication here.

89
00:06:12,030 --> 00:06:14,140
So, here there is a type mismatch.
89

90
00:06:14,430 --> 00:06:17,110
So, this is of integer type and this is
90

91
00:06:17,130 --> 00:06:18,630
so what is a type of this?
91

92
00:06:18,630 --> 00:06:22,590
It is not mentioned, if it is not mentioned then it is considered as double.
92

93
00:06:22,620 --> 00:06:24,770
So, integer multiplied by double
93

94
00:06:24,780 --> 00:06:27,300
the result is always promoted to double.
94

95
00:06:27,300 --> 00:06:30,130
So, then double is stored in integer type.
95

96
00:06:30,120 --> 00:06:35,440
So, only the integer part will be saved in tax, in tax variable,
96

97
00:06:35,550 --> 00:06:37,340
all the decimal part will be lost.

98
00:06:37,380 --> 00:06:40,080
So, we don't care about decimal part in this exercise.

99
00:06:40,110 --> 00:06:42,000
So, that's why, this would be fine,
99

100
00:06:42,000 --> 00:06:44,820
otherwise  you can also typecast this like this.

And let's execute. So, enter your total income

let me enter 9000 and tax payable is 0.

So, actually we missed

888, that's correct.

So, let's say, so there is a problem.

112

So here,

what you do is, if(temp_income  <  0) then you have to printf for example

115

income cannot be negative.

So because, this is invalid case.So, first let's try with positive numbers.

Works fine, let's try with

negative number.

Now it works.

So, try to reproduce this and I hope you understood about the if-else-if ladder and please note that, so

there is no else block here.

00:08:28,360 --> 00:08:30,630

that is NOP, you can use the else block at the end.

So, if none of these expressions are true, then at the end it will come to the else block and that is NOP.

131

So,
131

132

but that won't happen in our case because, at least this expression will be evaluated as true.

1
00:00:00,090 --> 00:00:01,760
Hey, welcome back to the lecture.
1

2
00:00:01,770 --> 00:00:07,770
So, now let's continue our discussion on decision making statements in 'C' and in this lecture
2

3
00:00:07,770 --> 00:00:11,550
let's explore about conditional operators. Conditional
3

4
00:00:11,550 --> 00:00:18,000
operator is a ternary operator in 'C' used for conditional evaluation.
4

5
00:00:18,000 --> 00:00:20,910
So, this is a symbol we use for the conditional operator.
5

6
00:00:21,510 --> 00:00:26,330
So, it's a ternary operator because it depends upon 3 operands.
6

7
00:00:26,460 --> 00:00:28,570
It operates on 3 operands.
7

8
00:00:28,650 --> 00:00:32,400
Let's explore the syntax of conditional operator.
8

9
00:00:32,430 --> 00:00:38,710
This is a conditional operator, so conditional operator is actually a collection of three operands.
9

10
00:00:38,730 --> 00:00:46,020
This is the first operand which may be a value or which may be an expression or it may be any 'C' statement
10

11
00:00:46,200 --> 00:00:54,120
like a function call or something like that. So, first operand followed by a question mark (?),
11

12
00:00:54,170 --> 00:00:58,070
and the second operand followed by a colon(:),
12

13
00:00:58,070 --> 00:01:00,820
and after that comes the third operand.
13

14
00:01:00,830 --> 00:01:04,220
Now, let's understand the evaluation of the conditional
14

15
00:01:04,220 --> 00:01:08,390
operator with an example. So, here is an example.
15

16
00:01:08,450 --> 00:01:10,330
So, here there is a variable a,
16

17
00:01:10,400 --> 00:01:15,080
and this is the conditional operation expression.
17

18
00:01:15,110 --> 00:01:17,400
So, here we can see that this is a operand1,
18

19
00:01:17,430 --> 00:01:26,040
this is a operand2, and this is a operand3. So, in this case what happens is that, first the expression1
19

20
00:01:26,040 --> 00:01:29,100
is evaluated or executed.
20

21
00:01:29,100 --> 00:01:37,160
So, when this is executed what happens here? 5 + 4 is 9.So, which is true, isn't it?
21

22
00:01:37,170 --> 00:01:47,340
So, if this operand is true, then this expression will be executed and the result of this expression will
22

23
00:01:47,340 --> 00:01:49,300
be saved in the variable a
23

24
00:01:49,380 --> 00:01:50,760
in this case.
24

25
00:01:50,760 --> 00:02:01,070
So, if the evaluation of this expression is false, then so this operand will be executed or evaluated
25

26
00:02:01,290 --> 00:02:04,380
and the result is saved into the 
26

27
00:02:04,380 --> 00:02:09,330
variable a. Very simple. If this is true then this is executed,
27

28
00:02:09,540 --> 00:02:12,350
if this is false then this is executed.
28

29
00:02:13,320 --> 00:02:18,440
So, in this case a = 5, because this is true, isn't it?
29

30
00:02:18,450 --> 00:02:19,370
So, here is a summary.
30

31
00:02:19,450 --> 00:02:27,400
So, this operand is executed first and if the result is true, then this will be the final result.
31

32
00:02:27,480 --> 00:02:32,540
So, if this expression evaluation is false, then this will be the final result.
32

33
00:02:33,540 --> 00:02:40,270
So, sometimes you can use conditional operator to replace if-else statements. For example, consider this
33

34
00:02:40,270 --> 00:02:41,350
code snippet here.
34

35
00:02:41,740 --> 00:02:49,000
So here, you are actually comparing the variable age with a value 18 here and then you are taking
35

36
00:02:49,000 --> 00:02:55,300
the decision. The whole code can be written in one line using conditional operator.
36

37
00:02:55,420 --> 00:02:56,840
So, here it is.
37

38
00:02:56,860 --> 00:03:00,670
So here,  first this expression is evaluated.
38

39
00:03:00,670 --> 00:03:07,570
So, if age is less than 18, if it is true then this expression will be executed.
39

40
00:03:07,600 --> 00:03:11,210
So, in this case the expression is a function call, that is printf.
40

41
00:03:11,530 --> 00:03:13,060
So, printf will be executed.
41

42
00:03:13,060 --> 00:03:16,060
So, please note that there is no semicolon at the end of printf.
42

43
00:03:16,510 --> 00:03:19,960
So because, this is a complete statement which ends here,
43

44
00:03:19,960 --> 00:03:23,030
so don't give intermediate semicolon.
44

45
00:03:23,080 --> 00:03:27,400
So now, if this evaluation is false, then this will be printed.
45

46
00:03:27,460 --> 00:03:32,570
So, in sometimes instead of using if and else you can use conditional operator.
46

47
00:03:32,580 --> 00:03:34,820
So, evaluate these expressions.
47

48
00:03:34,820 --> 00:03:36,990
So, here int a = 5.
48

49
00:03:37,000 --> 00:03:42,020
So, what is the result of a after execution of this statement?
49

50
00:03:42,040 --> 00:03:48,120
So here, just first check the first operand, so which is actually false, isn't it?
50

51
00:03:48,130 --> 00:03:54,050
So, if it is false this will be executed and assigned to the variable a.
51

52
00:03:54,070 --> 00:03:58,630
So, the equivalent code becomes a = a++.
52

53
00:03:58,630 --> 00:04:02,550
So, here a is five and this is a post-increment,
53

54
00:04:02,590 --> 00:04:05,650
so that's why, the value of a is actually 5.
54

55
00:04:06,040 --> 00:04:12,670
So, now some of you may be surprised or you may argue that a = 6, but that is not the
55

56
00:04:12,670 --> 00:04:14,110
case actually.
56

57
00:04:14,110 --> 00:04:17,150
So, let me explain how this executes.
57

58
00:04:17,230 --> 00:04:22,770
So, in this case that is a = a++, initial value of the a is 5.
58

59
00:04:22,780 --> 00:04:27,720
So, when we were discussing about the post-increment and pre-increment,
59

60
00:04:27,730 --> 00:04:29,010
so what did I say,
60

61
00:04:29,020 --> 00:04:35,130
So, first the value of this variable is assigned to the left hand side.
61

62
00:04:35,140 --> 00:04:39,880
Isn't it? a becomes 5 and after that a is incremented.
62

63
00:04:39,940 --> 00:04:43,680
So, it looks like the final value of the a is 6, isn't it?
63

64
00:04:43,990 --> 00:04:45,320
But that is not the case.
64

65
00:04:45,340 --> 00:04:53,590
So, to understand this we have to investigate how compiler generates instructions in the case of post-increment
65

66
00:04:53,680 --> 00:05:00,510
and in the case of pre-increment. So, first analyze how things work if the expression is something like
66

67
00:05:00,510 --> 00:05:04,060
this a = b++, let's say b = 5.
67

68
00:05:04,140 --> 00:05:08,750
So, behind the scene compiler generates instruction something like this.
68

69
00:05:08,820 --> 00:05:11,780
So, there will be a temporary space will be created,
69

70
00:05:11,790 --> 00:05:16,750
so I'm just explaining the behind the scene instructions a which is generated by the compiler.
70

71
00:05:16,860 --> 00:05:19,580
So, there will be a temporary space will be created,
71

72
00:05:19,590 --> 00:05:21,190
it may be a register or something
72

73
00:05:21,240 --> 00:05:23,690
and the value of b will be stored.
73

74
00:05:24,000 --> 00:05:28,740
And after that b is incremented by 1, b = b + 1.
74

75
00:05:28,740 --> 00:05:31,210
So, here temp is actually 5,
75

76
00:05:31,380 --> 00:05:39,180
so b is actually 6, and after that a is assigned with a value of temp.
76

77
00:05:39,600 --> 00:05:42,360
So, these are the behind the scene instructions.
77

78
00:05:42,840 --> 00:05:47,190
So, that's why, we say a = 5 in this case.
78

79
00:05:47,190 --> 00:05:50,520
So, now apply the same thing in the case of a++.
79

80
00:05:50,910 --> 00:05:58,290
So, temp= a,   a= a + 1, and then a = temp.
80

81
00:05:58,410 --> 00:06:00,780
So, temp was 5
81

82
00:06:00,990 --> 00:06:02,880
here a is 6,
82

83
00:06:02,880 --> 00:06:04,460
and again it becomes 5.
83

84
00:06:04,470 --> 00:06:08,160
So, that's why, the value of this expression is a = 5.
84

85
00:06:08,850 --> 00:06:11,100
So, now evaluate these expressions,
85

86
00:06:11,100 --> 00:06:12,920
now check this expression.
86

87
00:06:12,930 --> 00:06:14,830
So, what is a value of a?
87

88
00:06:14,850 --> 00:06:19,770
So, first of all this is a operand1 which must be evaluated first.
88

89
00:06:19,770 --> 00:06:21,930
So, a + b, which is 15.
89

90
00:06:22,290 --> 00:06:28,150
So, that means !(15). !(15) which is nothing but 0.
90

91
00:06:28,170 --> 00:06:31,010
So, if it is 0, a will be assigned to a.
91

92
00:06:31,020 --> 00:06:34,410
So, the value of a = 5 in this case.
92

93
00:06:34,410 --> 00:06:36,420
So, that's about conditional operators
93

94
00:06:36,420 --> 00:06:40,860
and in the next lecture let's learn about switch/case statement.

95
00:06:41,280 --> 00:06:42,540
And I will see you in the next lecture.

1
00:00:00,090 --> 00:00:07,090
In this lecture, let's discuss about switch/case statement. So, switch/case statement is one the decision
1

2
00:00:07,090 --> 00:00:08,650
making statement available
2

3
00:00:08,650 --> 00:00:13,920
in 'C'. It may be used instead of several if-else statements.
3

4
00:00:13,920 --> 00:00:17,300
Let's understand the syntax of switch/case statement.
4

5
00:00:17,410 --> 00:00:23,030
As I said, this is also used to take some decisions based on the expression evaluation.
5

6
00:00:23,110 --> 00:00:30,040
How the switch/case works is, first the expression will be evaluated and after that it is compared
6

7
00:00:30,040 --> 00:00:31,780
with several cases.
7

8
00:00:31,990 --> 00:00:38,680
So, a switch case body is actually collection of several cases and a default case.
8

9
00:00:39,220 --> 00:00:41,980
So, this is a optional default case.
9

10
00:00:41,980 --> 00:00:49,090
So, when none of the cases are true or matched with the expression evaluation, then the default case
10

11
00:00:49,090 --> 00:00:50,890
will be evaluated.
11

12
00:00:50,890 --> 00:00:54,060
So, now let's examine this switch/case block.
12

13
00:00:54,220 --> 00:00:58,510
First the expression will be evaluated in the switch statement.
13

14
00:00:58,510 --> 00:01:01,060
So, this is a switch statement.
14

15
00:01:01,180 --> 00:01:04,590
So, please note that switch, case, break,
15

16
00:01:04,750 --> 00:01:07,840
so all these are reserved keywords of 'C' standard.
16

17
00:01:08,710 --> 00:01:13,120
So, this is the body of the switch statement and inside the body
17

18
00:01:13,120 --> 00:01:21,760
you can have many case statements or case blocks and this is a default block. First the expression is
18

19
00:01:21,760 --> 00:01:28,740
evaluated and based on the final result of the expression a case block will be executed.
19

20
00:01:31,360 --> 00:01:39,850
And remember that, this is a case of this value and this value has to be an integer value remember.
20

21
00:01:39,970 --> 00:01:44,110
So, case must be followed by an integer value
21

22
00:01:44,440 --> 00:01:52,570
and after that you have to write a colon(:), and after that for that case you can write as many number of
22

23
00:01:52,570 --> 00:01:53,790
statements here.
23

24
00:01:54,100 --> 00:02:02,850
And once all the statements are executed, you can break the switch statement by using a break keyword.
24

25
00:02:02,920 --> 00:02:10,270
So, when break is executed, the control comes out of the switch body and it continues with the rest of
25

26
00:02:10,270 --> 00:02:11,300
the code.
26

27
00:02:11,320 --> 00:02:12,970
Let's see one example.
27

28
00:02:13,000 --> 00:02:21,050
So, here is a program which reads a keypad and based on the key_read the decision will be taken.
28

29
00:02:21,100 --> 00:02:28,670
So, this read_keypad function returns the value of the key that the user has pressed.
29

30
00:02:28,720 --> 00:02:32,350
Let's say user presses the key 2, so it returns 2.
30

31
00:02:32,410 --> 00:02:40,210
So, 2 is stored in the key_read variable. And that key _read variable is used as an expression in the
31

32
00:02:40,210 --> 00:02:41,910
switch statement.
32

33
00:02:41,950 --> 00:02:44,980
So, let's say this is 2. The value of key_read is 2.
33

34
00:02:45,010 --> 00:02:52,220
So, that's why, this value is compared with values of the case, case statements.
34

35
00:02:52,270 --> 00:03:00,050
So, that's why, in this case if this is 2, then control directly comes to this case. So, it doesn't execute
35

36
00:03:00,100 --> 00:03:07,570
case 1, it directly goes to the case 2. And in case 2 it executes the statements of case 2.
36

37
00:03:07,570 --> 00:03:12,120
So, in this case this is a function call to turn on all the LEDs.
37

38
00:03:12,130 --> 00:03:18,280
So, once this function is executed the program actually encounters break.
38

39
00:03:18,280 --> 00:03:27,250
So, once break is encountered the control immediately breaks this body and it moves out of the switch
39

40
00:03:27,250 --> 00:03:30,280
body and continues for the rest of the code of the program.
40

41
00:03:30,760 --> 00:03:37,750
So, let's say this is 2 and let's say this break is not present, in that case what happens is the switch
41

42
00:03:37,750 --> 00:03:39,460
body will not be broken,
42

43
00:03:39,520 --> 00:03:47,620
it continues with this statement and then it finds a break here. And once it finds a break, then it
43

44
00:03:47,770 --> 00:03:51,210
breaks the switch body and goes out of the switch body.
44

45
00:03:51,310 --> 00:03:55,180
So, sometimes we use break and sometimes we don't use a break,
45

46
00:03:55,180 --> 00:04:00,550
so that depends upon your logic, but not using a break statement is completely fine
46

47
00:04:00,550 --> 00:04:02,910
that is not actually compilation error.
47

48
00:04:03,160 --> 00:04:04,740
So, carefully use break
48

49
00:04:04,750 --> 00:04:06,110
that's according to your logic,
49

50
00:04:06,160 --> 00:04:09,180
whether to use break or whether not to use break.
50

51
00:04:09,190 --> 00:04:15,040
So, let's say this key_read value is 5, so then none of this cases actually match, isn't it?
51

52
00:04:15,100 --> 00:04:18,390
So, that's why, the control directly goes to the default case
52

53
00:04:18,460 --> 00:04:20,810
and this will be executed.
53

54
00:04:20,860 --> 00:04:22,480
So, these statements will be executed
54

55
00:04:22,480 --> 00:04:28,840
and after that, so for a default case you need not to mention the break statement, because it anyway comes
55

56
00:04:28,840 --> 00:04:29,470
at the end.
56

57
00:04:29,830 --> 00:04:30,840
So, you should always write
57

58
00:04:30,840 --> 00:04:31,280
default
58

59
00:04:31,280 --> 00:04:32,320
case at the end
59

60
00:04:32,320 --> 00:04:35,340
and it goes out of the switch body.
60

61
00:04:35,500 --> 00:04:41,710
So, I hope you got the idea behind the switch/case statement and it will be more clear when we do one
61

62
00:04:41,710 --> 00:04:43,660
exercise and will see in the next lecture.


━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

video 13

0
1
00:00:00,090 --> 00:00:01,320
Hey, welcome back to the lecture.
1

2
00:00:01,350 --> 00:00:08,140
So, now let's do one exercise and we'll do this exercise by using a switch/case statement.
2

3
00:00:08,190 --> 00:00:14,340
So, the exercise is write a program to calculate the area of different geometrical figures like Circle,
3

4
00:00:14,340 --> 00:00:18,080
triangle, trapezoid, circle, and rectangle.
4

5
00:00:18,090 --> 00:00:24,540
The program should ask the user to enter the code for which the user wants to find out the area.
5

6
00:00:24,540 --> 00:00:32,280
These are the codes. A 't' for triangle, 'z' for trapezoid ,'c' for circle, 's' for square, 'r' for rectangle.
6

7
00:00:33,030 --> 00:00:36,190
So, lets say user enters the code 't'.
7

8
00:00:36,480 --> 00:00:37,980
So, that means it's a triangle.
8

9
00:00:38,000 --> 00:00:42,180
So, for to calculate the area of the triangle you need more information.
9

10
00:00:42,210 --> 00:00:48,660
You need to know the value of base and height. So, you should ask the user to enter the values of base
10

11
00:00:48,660 --> 00:00:53,580
and height, and then you have to calculate the area, and then you have to print the area like that.
11

12
00:00:53,580 --> 00:00:59,220
If the code is 'z', then it is for trapezoid and you have to ask base1, base2, height, and you have to
12

13
00:00:59,220 --> 00:01:02,060
calculate the area according to these formula.
13

14
00:01:02,820 --> 00:01:06,430
So, this is a area of a rectangle.
14

15
00:01:06,480 --> 00:01:09,310
So, let me show you the sample output.
15

16
00:01:09,900 --> 00:01:16,150
So, I have implemented the program and first the program should prompt this message to the user with
16

17
00:01:16,150 --> 00:01:17,280
the codes.
17

18
00:01:17,280 --> 00:01:19,460
So, now let's say user enter the code
18

19
00:01:19,470 --> 00:01:22,320
c, then
19

20
00:01:22,340 --> 00:01:27,710
so this is circle area calculation and it has to ask the user to enter the radius
20

21
00:01:27,710 --> 00:01:28,640
in this case.
21

22
00:01:28,640 --> 00:01:34,180
So, let's say, user enters -9 and here we can see that it prints
22

23
00:01:34,190 --> 00:01:35,900
Radius cannot be negative.
23

24
00:01:36,050 --> 00:01:38,390
So, all these error handling you should take care.
24

25
00:01:38,420 --> 00:01:44,660
So, now let's type the code t, so triangle area calculation. Enter the base value 
25

26
00:01:44,660 --> 00:01:48,580
let me type 9, enter the height value let me type
26

27
00:01:48,590 --> 00:01:49,320
.7
27

28
00:01:49,910 --> 00:01:55,780
So, this is a area. So, try to implement this for all other geometrical figures. In the next lecture
28

29
00:01:55,790 --> 00:01:59,660
I will implement this using switch/case statements and I will see you in the next lecture.

2
00:00:06,930 --> 00:00:08,550
using switch/case statements.
2

3
00:00:08,550 --> 00:00:14,410
So, here I am ready with my project and the project name is switchCaseArea.

4
00:00:14,520 --> 00:00:19,050
Let's start coding. First of all we have to prompt a message to the user.
4

5
00:00:19,470 --> 00:00:20,550
So, let's do that.
5

6
00:00:23,400 --> 00:00:27,900
So, let me print area calculation program and after that let me print the codes.
6

7
00:00:30,380 --> 00:00:31,120
Circle
7

8
00:00:31,130 --> 00:00:32,900
the code is c
8

9
00:00:33,020 --> 00:00:36,940
and let me give \n to go to the next line. So, for
9

10
00:00:36,980 --> 00:00:38,830
triangle t, trapezoid
10

11
00:00:38,870 --> 00:00:43,320
it is z, square s, and rectangle r.
11

12
00:00:44,240 --> 00:00:44,850
And after that
12

13
00:00:44,870 --> 00:00:48,930
let me print "Enter the code here".
13

14
00:00:50,110 --> 00:00:59,370
After that I have to receive the code. For that, I would use scanf("%c", &code)
14

15
00:00:59,720 --> 00:01:02,530
and I would use code as a char
15

16
00:01:02,540 --> 00:01:06,630
or you can use int8_t code.
16

17
00:01:07,740 --> 00:01:16,550
So, int8_t is nothing but char. And after that let's use switch case switch(code), because
17

18
00:01:16,580 --> 00:01:19,310
we are comparing the code now, switch(code).
18

19
00:01:19,460 --> 00:01:23,790
So, let's open the curly brace and hit enter.
19

20
00:01:23,900 --> 00:01:27,830
You can see that the cursor is automatically indented.
20

21
00:01:27,830 --> 00:01:29,850
So, start writing your cases now.
21

22
00:01:29,900 --> 00:01:32,310
Case, first case is for the code
22

23
00:01:32,330 --> 00:01:33,060
c.
23

24
00:01:33,350 --> 00:01:35,900
So, you can't write just c here.
24

25
00:01:35,900 --> 00:01:36,230
Why?
25

26
00:01:36,230 --> 00:01:40,100
Because, the label of the case must be an integer value.
26

27
00:01:40,100 --> 00:01:44,600
So, we have to compare the ASCII value of the entered character.
27

28
00:01:45,140 --> 00:01:50,220
So, the value of the code is nothing but the ASCII value of the entered character.
28

29
00:01:50,270 --> 00:01:54,400
So, that's why, we have to compare with the ASCII values of the codes.
29

30
00:01:54,710 --> 00:01:59,720
So, that's why, we have to use c inside the single quotation.
30

31
00:01:59,780 --> 00:02:04,490
So, that will give us the ASCII value of the character 'c' which is nothing but an integer.
31

32
00:02:05,030 --> 00:02:11,160
After that you have to give a colon (:), and after that hit enter, and here let's type
32

33
00:02:11,210 --> 00:02:18,160
printf(" Circle Area calculation"). Let's print that.
33

34
00:02:18,390 --> 00:02:24,290
And after that you have to ask for the radius, that looks good.
34

35
00:02:24,290 --> 00:02:27,130
And after that you have to read whatever user enters,
35

36
00:02:27,130 --> 00:02:33,220
so again let's use scanf, %f I would use, because user may enter a real number.
36

37
00:02:33,230 --> 00:02:36,240
So, %f let's say radius.
37

38
00:02:36,260 --> 00:02:37,900
So, &radius.
38

39
00:02:38,000 --> 00:02:45,410
So, I didn't define this variable so let's define that radius or I will just say 'r'. So, now let me just
39

40
00:02:45,410 --> 00:02:47,170
create a variable r,
40

41
00:02:47,480 --> 00:02:54,940
let me also create some variables for base, height etc.. Because we may need them, so float r b h.
41

42
00:02:55,010 --> 00:02:59,580
And after that let me create another variable to hold the area, float area.
42

43
00:02:59,750 --> 00:03:05,110
That looks good. And after that here area is equal to pi, r sqaure.
43

44
00:03:05,770 --> 00:03:12,740
So, let me use pi as 3.1415 * r * r. That's it. 
44

45
00:03:12,850 --> 00:03:18,260
And after that let's break. So, just use break statement with semicolon.
45

46
00:03:18,260 --> 00:03:21,090
So, that completes one case block.
46

47
00:03:21,400 --> 00:03:23,070
Let's implement another case block,
47

48
00:03:23,330 --> 00:03:26,030
so for that hit enter and when you type
48

49
00:03:26,030 --> 00:03:29,300
case here it will automatically get indented. Again
49

50
00:03:29,300 --> 00:03:35,210
remember that indentation is not compulsory in 'C', it will not cause you any logical error or syntax error.
50

51
00:03:35,810 --> 00:03:40,160
So, indentation just makes your code look good and proper.
51

52
00:03:40,190 --> 00:03:48,140
So, now let's check for a 't', 't' and again let me copy all these things here.
52

53
00:03:48,140 --> 00:03:57,680
So, this is triangle area calculation enter a base, base value and scanf for base.
53

54
00:03:57,680 --> 00:04:01,580
And after that again repeat this for height,
54

55
00:04:04,980 --> 00:04:11,130
so h. So, area is b * h divided by 2.
55

56
00:04:11,250 --> 00:04:18,410
After that, let's copy this and create one more case for 'z'.
56

57
00:04:18,480 --> 00:04:19,500
So, like that.
57

58
00:04:19,500 --> 00:04:25,770
So, you have to complete for all other codes, I'll quickly finish this and I'll get back to you.
58

59
00:04:25,770 --> 00:04:29,850
So, I just completed all the cases 'c' ,'t', 'z', 's', and 'r'.
59

60
00:04:30,450 --> 00:04:38,340
If user enters any other characters or codes, then you should print something like it's an invalid input.
60

61
00:04:39,010 --> 00:04:41,760
So, for that, let's make use of default case.
61

62
00:04:41,910 --> 00:04:47,810
So, let's create a default case, just type default and give a colon is a default case.
62

63
00:04:47,850 --> 00:04:52,560
So, in the default case let's write invalid input.
63

64
00:04:52,880 --> 00:04:58,740
That's it. You need not to mention any break statement here, anyway when it hits default it executes this
64

65
00:04:58,860 --> 00:05:02,120
and it goes out of the body of the switch.
65

66
00:05:02,130 --> 00:05:07,020
So, this is actually end of body of switch.
66

67
00:05:07,410 --> 00:05:08,790
That looks perfect.
67

68
00:05:09,000 --> 00:05:14,100
This code doesn't handle negative numbers, because while calculating area you should not enter negative
68

69
00:05:14,100 --> 00:05:14,520
values.
69

70
00:05:14,760 --> 00:05:17,110
So, you have to take care of that.
70

71
00:05:17,160 --> 00:05:19,410
After that, you have to print the area.
71

72
00:05:20,280 --> 00:05:28,840
So here, let's make area as -1 here. So because, we can compare the value of the area. So, once control
72

73
00:05:28,840 --> 00:05:31,510
comes out of the switch case you have to print the area.
73

74
00:05:31,780 --> 00:05:40,120
So, if(area < 0), then that means that something went wrong either user has entered non valid
74

75
00:05:40,150 --> 00:05:43,800
input or a user has entered negative numbers.
75

76
00:05:43,800 --> 00:05:47,140
So, in that case we'll consider area as negative.
76

77
00:05:47,140 --> 00:05:51,420
So, if(area < 0), then you should not print the area.
77

78
00:05:51,430 --> 00:06:01,480
So, let me just give semicolon NOP. Else a printf ("Area = %f\n", and area).
78

79
00:06:01,570 --> 00:06:02,190
That's it.
79

80
00:06:02,200 --> 00:06:03,830
So, instead of writing this
80

81
00:06:03,880 --> 00:06:05,850
so this actually looks odd.
81

82
00:06:05,860 --> 00:06:12,390
So, what we can do is, we can rewrite this something like this. If(area < 0)
82

83
00:06:12,820 --> 00:06:18,140
if it is not true, we can use not operate here. So, then print this.
83

84
00:06:18,160 --> 00:06:22,180
So, let me remove all this, so we can write something like this.
84

85
00:06:22,300 --> 00:06:24,540
So, if(area < 0)
85

86
00:06:24,610 --> 00:06:27,970
if it is true, then not of true is false.
86

87
00:06:27,970 --> 00:06:29,710
So, this will not be executed.
87

88
00:06:30,190 --> 00:06:35,410
So, if(area < 0) if it is false, then false of not is true.
88

89
00:06:35,470 --> 00:06:37,580
So, that's why, this will get executed.
89

90
00:06:37,600 --> 00:06:42,560
Let's not give any space here, so this looks good.
90

91
00:06:42,610 --> 00:06:44,830
So, now let's test this application.

92

Let's see how it works.

So, let me just compile this code, so no errors.So, area calculation program

so I would like to go for 's' square, so enter side value 8,

00:01:01,050 --> 00:01:03,950

00:01:04,080 --> 00:01:16,200

00:01:18,120 --> 00:01:30,990

00:01:32,040 --> 00:01:32,850

31
00:01:47,670 --> 00:01:51,600
So, (a < 0) || (b < 0) || (h < 0).
31

32
00:01:51,720 --> 00:01:57,660
So, if one among these become negative, then this expression will be true.
32

33
00:01:57,660 --> 00:02:06,350
So, that's why, we are going to print this. So, if(a  <  0) then side cannot be negative and
33

34
00:02:06,350 --> 00:02:11,470
this is the area. And also do for rectangle
34

35
00:02:11,470 --> 00:02:17,570
so that completes our error handling for negative numbers, let's execute.
35

36
00:02:17,570 --> 00:02:21,800
So, first let me try rectangle. So, enter width value.
36

37
00:02:21,800 --> 00:02:26,150
Let me type 4 and let me type negative number now -5,
37

38
00:02:26,600 --> 00:02:28,240
so width or length cannot be negative.
38

39
00:02:28,340 --> 00:02:29,630
That's working fine.
39

40
00:02:33,160 --> 00:02:37,320
So, that completes the discussion on switch/case statements
40

41
00:02:37,350 --> 00:02:42,320
and so we are going to use switch case statements in our embedded programming as well.
41

42
00:02:42,460 --> 00:02:45,730
So, that's all about a switch/case statement as of now.
42

43
00:02:45,730 --> 00:02:48,850
And we'll see more exercises as we make a progress
43

44
00:02:48,880 --> 00:02:55,000
and from the next lecture onwards I'm going to cover looping in 'C'. I will see you in the next lecture.

section 17---Bitwise Operators


video 1

0
1
00:00:00,760 --> 00:00:02,090
Hey, welcome back to the lecture.
1

2
00:00:02,090 --> 00:00:09,140
So, from this lecture onwards let's explore about the bitwise operators in 'C', which is heavily used in
2

3
00:00:09,140 --> 00:00:15,590
embedded system programming because most of the time in embedded system programming using 'C', we end up manipulating,
3

4
00:00:15,620 --> 00:00:21,340
the memory addresses, the contents of the peripheral registers, status registers etc..
4

5
00:00:21,370 --> 00:00:30,280
So, in that cases so bitwise operators are used and it is one of the important topic in Embedded 'C' programming.
5

6
00:00:30,320 --> 00:00:37,330
So, now let's explore all the bitwise operators presents in 'C'. There are 6 bit wise operators.
6

7
00:00:37,430 --> 00:00:40,540
So, and these are the respective symbols.
7

8
00:00:40,640 --> 00:00:47,630
So, we have bitwise AND, bitwise OR, bitwise left shift, right shift, bitwise NOT
8

9
00:00:47,660 --> 00:00:51,910
so this is also called as negation and bitwise XOR.
9

10
00:00:52,540 --> 00:00:54,860
Let's explore them one by one.
10

11
00:00:54,860 --> 00:00:59,930
First of all, what is the difference between a Logical operator and a bitwise operator?
11

12
00:01:00,470 --> 00:01:03,510
So, for example, if you use double ampersand(&&)
12

13
00:01:03,590 --> 00:01:09,860
so this is actually a 'logical AND' operator. If you use single ampersand(&)
13

14
00:01:09,860 --> 00:01:15,070
this is 'bitwise AND' operator. For example, to know how the operation is different
14

15
00:01:15,080 --> 00:01:19,730
let's take an example, let's say A = 40 and B =30.
15

16
00:01:19,730 --> 00:01:20,860
So, what happens here?
16

17
00:01:20,870 --> 00:01:24,260
So, in this case A is true, B is true.
17

18
00:01:24,260 --> 00:01:27,940
So, the exact value of the A and B are not considered here.
18

19
00:01:27,980 --> 00:01:33,880
So, only thing which matters is whether the operands are 0 or non-zero.
19

20
00:01:33,890 --> 00:01:40,170
So, since both of these operands are non-zero, so the result will be true in this case.
20

21
00:01:40,190 --> 00:01:42,190
So, c = 1
21

22
00:01:42,470 --> 00:01:46,880
in this case. Now, how about this operation?
22

23
00:01:46,880 --> 00:01:48,050
A  bitwise
23

24
00:01:48,200 --> 00:01:49,850
AND with B.
24

25
00:01:49,850 --> 00:01:51,310
Let's explore that.
25

26
00:01:51,380 --> 00:01:53,000
Let's see how this works.
26

27
00:01:53,060 --> 00:01:54,650
In this case, we do
27

28
00:01:54,770 --> 00:01:55,950
AND operation
28

29
00:01:55,970 --> 00:01:57,020
bit by bit.
29

30
00:01:57,020 --> 00:02:00,430
So, that's why, it is called as  bitwise operation. Here,
30

31
00:02:00,440 --> 00:02:05,480
this is actually the binary format of the number 40,
31

32
00:02:05,600 --> 00:02:09,130
and this is a binary format of the number 30.
32

33
00:02:09,680 --> 00:02:15,890
So now, A & B is nothing but you have to perform AND operation
33

34
00:02:15,890 --> 00:02:19,310
bit by bit. So, 0  & 0 is 0.
34

35
00:02:19,520 --> 00:02:21,480
So, all these things are 0.
35

36
00:02:21,500 --> 00:02:23,770
So, only here it turns out to be 1.
36

37
00:02:24,350 --> 00:02:27,970
So, C is equal to in this case is 8.
37

38
00:02:28,010 --> 00:02:29,450
So, here C was 1
38

39
00:02:29,480 --> 00:02:31,280
and in this case C = 8.
39

40
00:02:31,760 --> 00:02:35,630
So, that means the takeaway from this lecture is very simple.
40

41
00:02:35,630 --> 00:02:40,540
So, in bitwise operation you do the operation like AND, OR, etc.
41

42
00:02:40,580 --> 00:02:41,870
bit by bit.
42

43
00:02:41,870 --> 00:02:44,600
So, let's take an example with OR.
43

44
00:02:44,630 --> 00:02:46,700
So, this is logical OR operation,
44

45
00:02:46,730 --> 00:02:49,010
so in this case C =1.
45

46
00:02:49,040 --> 00:02:54,080
So, how about this? So, you have to do the OR operation bit by bit.
46

47
00:02:54,170 --> 00:02:56,210
So, this turns out to be 62.
47

48
00:02:56,270 --> 00:03:03,100
So, C = 62 in this case. Let's explore about the bitwise NOT operator.
48

49
00:03:03,100 --> 00:03:09,950
So, this is a bitwise NOT operator which is an unary operator, so because it needs only one operand.
49

50
00:03:10,180 --> 00:03:15,980
So, this is how you use the bitwise NOT operator. So, you cannot write A negation.
50

51
00:03:16,090 --> 00:03:17,940
You cannot write like this.
51

52
00:03:18,040 --> 00:03:21,710
So, this is a operand should come after this operator.
52

53
00:03:21,970 --> 00:03:24,070
So, this is also called as negation
53

54
00:03:24,080 --> 00:03:27,220
operator. Let's see what is the value of C in this case.
54

55
00:03:27,220 --> 00:03:29,810
So, C = ~A.
55

56
00:03:29,860 --> 00:03:36,020
So, this is A, the ~ A is you have to change zeros into ones and ones into zeros.
56

57
00:03:36,070 --> 00:03:39,970
So, that's why, in this case C = -41.
57

58
00:03:40,210 --> 00:03:48,940
Because, this is signed variable. So, that's about bitwise AND, bitwise OR, and bitwise NOT,
58

59
00:03:49,010 --> 00:03:50,790
which is also called as negation
59

60
00:03:50,790 --> 00:03:53,060
operator. Let's see the XOR.
60

61
00:03:53,450 --> 00:03:55,300
So, there is no logical XOR.
61

62
00:03:55,490 --> 00:03:58,140
So, there is only bitwise XOR operator,
62

63
00:03:58,220 --> 00:04:03,670
so this is a symbol you have to use and this does bit by bit XORing.
63

64
00:04:03,680 --> 00:04:11,240
So, this is a truth table of XOR. In the case of XORing the output will be 1 only if the 2 operand 
64

65
00:04:11,240 --> 00:04:12,250
are different.
65

66
00:04:12,290 --> 00:04:16,630
So, consider this case here. So, here A and B are different,
66

67
00:04:16,680 --> 00:04:17,240
this is 0
67

68
00:04:17,250 --> 00:04:18,990
and this is 1. In this case, this is 1
68

69
00:04:19,020 --> 00:04:19,720
and this is 0.
69

70
00:04:20,280 --> 00:04:27,330
So, when both operands are different, then only output will be 1 and if both operands are equal, then output
70

71
00:04:27,330 --> 00:04:27,760
is 0.
71

72
00:04:27,920 --> 00:04:29,210
So, that's how, you should understand
72

73
00:04:29,210 --> 00:04:29,650
XOR.
73

74
00:04:29,730 --> 00:04:33,390
So, we'll see how we can use this in the programming.
74

75
00:04:33,390 --> 00:04:40,630
So, when we take up examples and let's understand the applicability of bitwise operations.
75

76
00:04:40,730 --> 00:04:47,920
So, in an Embedded C program, most of the time you'll be doing, testing of bits, setting of bits, clearing of bits,
76

77
00:04:48,010 --> 00:04:49,830
and toggling of bits.
77

78
00:04:49,840 --> 00:04:56,290
So, because let's say your LED is connected to one of the pin of the microcontroller port.
78

79
00:04:56,320 --> 00:04:59,690
So, if you want to turn on the LED you are going to set some bit,
79

80
00:05:00,190 --> 00:05:06,010
and if you want to turn off the LED you are going to clear some bits, and if you want to analyze the
80

81
00:05:06,010 --> 00:05:11,050
status register of a peripheral so then you'll be using a  testing of bits.
81

82
00:05:11,050 --> 00:05:17,290
So, in all those cases we'll be using the bitwise operators and when we do exercises we'll explore
82

83
00:05:17,290 --> 00:05:18,240
them.
83

84
00:05:18,250 --> 00:05:20,030
So, let's go step by step.
84

85
00:05:20,050 --> 00:05:23,230
So now, first let's do an exercise on the host machine,
85

86
00:05:23,230 --> 00:05:30,990
after that we can move on to the target. So now, write a program which takes 2 integers from the user,
86

87
00:05:31,200 --> 00:05:38,410
computes bitwise &, bitwise |, bitwise ^, and ~ and prints the result.
87

88
00:05:38,430 --> 00:05:41,210
So, I wanted to do this on your paper first.
88

89

Take a pen and paper and do the calculations. And then write a program and then compare the output of
89

90

the program with your calculations.

91

1
00:00:00,090 --> 00:00:02,310

2
00:00:02,460 --> 00:00:05,370
So, I am actually asking for two numbers here.

3
00:00:05,370 --> 00:00:06,730
So, I'm just taking here

4

signed int of width 32 bits. Bitwise AND : %d\n, num1,

&num2.

video 3

0
1
00:00:00,090 --> 00:00:06,540
So, in this lecture let's understand about testing of bits using bitwise & operator. So, we'll understand
1

2
00:00:06,540 --> 00:00:08,450
this by doing an exercise.
2

3
00:00:08,740 --> 00:00:14,770
So, write a program to find out whether a user entered a number is even or odd.
3

4
00:00:14,970 --> 00:00:22,860
Print an appropriate message on the console, and use testing of bits logic. To find out whether a number
4

5
00:00:22,860 --> 00:00:23,750
is even or odd,
5

6
00:00:23,760 --> 00:00:28,570
there are many logic you can build. But, we will use a testing of bits logic.
6

7
00:00:28,570 --> 00:00:30,900
So, how this works is, very simple
7

8
00:00:30,900 --> 00:00:32,710
let's consider a number
8

9
00:00:32,820 --> 00:00:35,820
an integer, let's say 46 .46
9

10
00:00:35,820 --> 00:00:45,770
if you write that in binary form you get this, and just check what exactly is the least significant bit.
10

11
00:00:46,220 --> 00:00:47,330
For an even number
11

12
00:00:47,330 --> 00:00:49,170
the LSB is always 0.
12

13
00:00:49,250 --> 00:00:51,440
So, now consider odd number.
13

14
00:00:51,440 --> 00:00:54,890
So, for the odd number the LSB  will be 1 always.
14

15
00:00:55,010 --> 00:01:03,020
So, that's why, by checking or by testing the least significant bit of a number we can find out whether
15

16
00:01:03,080 --> 00:01:05,660
a number is even or odd.
16

17
00:01:05,660 --> 00:01:10,540
So, that's a logic. So, we understood that we want to test a bit,
17

18
00:01:10,540 --> 00:01:13,030
so in this case it is LSB. How to do that?
18

19
00:01:13,570 --> 00:01:19,590
So, to do that we use a technique called a bit masking. A bit masking is a technique in programming
19

20
00:01:19,600 --> 00:01:24,160
used to test or modify the states of the bits of a given data.
20

21
00:01:24,160 --> 00:01:33,490
So here, we use bit masking  with a mask value, so we have to create a mask value. So, here we have to
21

22
00:01:33,580 --> 00:01:40,900
decide what should be the mask value and then take that mask value and do a bitwise & operation with
22

23
00:01:40,900 --> 00:01:43,210
a given data to get the output.
23

24
00:01:43,210 --> 00:01:45,090
So now, let's analyze how this works.
24

25
00:01:45,090 --> 00:01:50,560
All right. So, let's say this is the input number, so which is written in a binary format.
25

26
00:01:50,560 --> 00:01:53,450
We divide this number into two areas.
26

27
00:01:53,590 --> 00:01:59,910
So, this I call as area1 and this is  area2. Here, in this number
27

28
00:01:59,950 --> 00:02:07,360
the area1 is of no interest to us, isn't it? Because, our goal is to test a bit which falls under area
28

29
00:02:07,390 --> 00:02:08,350
2.
29

30
00:02:08,350 --> 00:02:11,380
So, our goal is to find out a number is even or odd.
30

31
00:02:11,530 --> 00:02:14,290
So, that's why, we care only about the LSB. 
31

32
00:02:14,620 --> 00:02:17,480
So, that's why, area1 is of no interest to us
32

33
00:02:17,500 --> 00:02:21,990
so 0 them out by using the mask value zeros. So, at the output
33

34
00:02:22,030 --> 00:02:23,980
the area1 is zeroed out.
34

35
00:02:23,980 --> 00:02:32,810
So, that's a reason why mask value is 0 for that area. So, we use a mask value 0 and we do bitwise
35

36
00:02:32,930 --> 00:02:34,580
AND with the number.
36

37
00:02:34,580 --> 00:02:41,840
So but, for the area1 I can't use 0 here. If I use 0, so then doesn't matter what is there in the area2
37

38
00:02:41,870 --> 00:02:43,100
output will be 0.
38

39
00:02:43,100 --> 00:02:52,320
So, that's why, what I do is, for the area2 we use the mask bit as 1, because I want to test this bit.
39

40
00:02:52,670 --> 00:02:57,650
So, that's why, to test a bit we use one with the bitwise & operator.
40

41
00:02:57,950 --> 00:03:00,910
So, that's why, if this position is 0, then I get 0 here.
41

42
00:03:01,010 --> 00:03:02,290
So, the output is 0.
42

43
00:03:02,360 --> 00:03:06,220
So, if the output is 0, then I conclude that the number is even.
43

44
00:03:06,560 --> 00:03:09,560
So, if the number is odd, then this position will be 1.
44

45
00:03:09,560 --> 00:03:12,460
So, 1 ANDed with 1 gives me 1.
45

46
00:03:12,470 --> 00:03:14,390
So, this output is non-zero.
46

47
00:03:14,480 --> 00:03:21,780
So, if the output is non-zero I will conclude that number is odd. The takeaway from this lecture is, if you
47

48
00:03:21,780 --> 00:03:25,620
want to test a bit so use bitwise & operation.
48

49
00:03:25,770 --> 00:03:29,750
And by using this technique you can test any bit position you want.
49

50
00:03:29,760 --> 00:03:33,170
For example, here I am checking the bit position 1.So,
50

51
00:03:33,210 --> 00:03:39,070
that's why, all these bits are 0 and I make only this bit position as 1.
51

52
00:03:39,180 --> 00:03:44,080
And here I am actually testing the bit position that is MSP
52

53
00:03:44,130 --> 00:03:47,330
and here we are testing the first two bit positions.
53

54
00:03:49,140 --> 00:03:55,530
Now, let's try to solve our problem that is finding out whether a number is even or odd.
54

55
00:03:55,560 --> 00:03:58,030
So, this is a logic I would like to use.
55

56
00:03:58,050 --> 00:04:05,640
So, here number is bitwise ANDed with mask value which is 1. If this whole expression turns out to be a
56

57
00:04:05,640 --> 00:04:08,730
non-zero value, so then it will be true.
57

58
00:04:08,730 --> 00:04:17,510
So, in that case print number as odd. So, if the output of this expression is false that is 0, then you
58

59
00:04:17,510 --> 00:04:19,950
can print the number as even.
59

60
00:04:20,280 --> 00:04:24,140
So, try to do this exercise by yourself and I'll see you in the next lecture.

00:00:04,200 --> 00:00:11,600
So, I am actually just asking for a number which is actually an integer, because even or odd refers to only
3

00:00:11,620 --> 00:00:15,410
integers, isn't it? Whether it is a negative integer or positive integer.
4

5
00:00:15,450 --> 00:00:17,710
So, that's why, I created a signed variable.
5

6
00:00:18,480 --> 00:00:25,650
So, now let's do our logic, if num1 & of mask value is 1.
6

7
00:00:25,710 --> 00:00:32,460
So, if this is non-zero, then %d is odd number,
7

8

else %d is even number.

9
00:00:37,520 --> 00:00:38,630

so let's say -244 is an even number.

0 is even number, 445 odd number.

13
00:00:49,590 --> 00:00:54,670
So, that's actually how you use AND operator to test the bits.

of a given number and print the result.

20

21
00:01:35,460 --> 00:01:41,640
and 7th bit position as 1 of that number, and then recalculate the value, and then write a program to
21

22
00:01:41,640 --> 00:01:44,270
compare your calculation. And I will see you  in the next lecture.

0
1
00:00:00,060 --> 00:00:02,290
So, let's do this exercise.
1

2
00:00:02,310 --> 00:00:06,440
So, now in this exercise we have to set only 4th and 7th
2

3
00:00:06,480 --> 00:00:10,920
bit position of a number, we should not affect any other bit positions
3

4
00:00:10,980 --> 00:00:12,680
we should keep that in mind.
4

5
00:00:12,870 --> 00:00:16,420
So, let's analyze this how we can set the bits.
5

6
00:00:16,620 --> 00:00:18,140
So, let's take a data.
6

7
00:00:18,180 --> 00:00:20,320
So, now let's say this is a data given data.
7

8
00:00:20,730 --> 00:00:25,320
So, for this we have to use a mask value, and let's say I use the mask value
8

9
00:00:25,350 --> 00:00:29,890
this one, where the 4th and 7th bits are set.
9

10
00:00:29,970 --> 00:00:36,950
After that we have to decide whether you want to use bitwise & operator or bitwise | operator.
10

11
00:00:37,080 --> 00:00:40,020
What happens if we use bitwise & operator?
11

12
00:00:40,020 --> 00:00:41,790
This is a result you get.
12

13
00:00:41,790 --> 00:00:44,540
So, here you can see that the output is not correct.
13

14
00:00:44,580 --> 00:00:46,700
So, we actually messed up a lot of bits here.
14

15
00:00:46,920 --> 00:00:51,820
So, we actually messed up with those bits, which we are not supposed to touch.
15

16
00:00:51,870 --> 00:00:55,800
So, it clearly shows that you cannot use AND operator,
16

17
00:00:55,860 --> 00:00:57,900
that is bitwise & operator
17

18
00:00:57,900 --> 00:01:01,350
if your requirement is to set the bits.
18

19
00:01:01,980 --> 00:01:07,620
So, that's why, the bitwise & operator is used to 'TEST' not to 'SET'.
19

20
00:01:07,620 --> 00:01:10,830
So, in this case you have to use bitwise | operator.
20

21
00:01:10,830 --> 00:01:12,080
So, this is not correct.
21

22
00:01:12,090 --> 00:01:14,330
So, you have to use bitwise | operator
22

23
00:01:14,760 --> 00:01:17,330
and this is the mask value in this case.
23

24
00:01:17,340 --> 00:01:24,120
So, the mask value turns out to be how much? so the 
mask value = 0x90,  isn't
24

25
00:01:24,120 --> 00:01:24,280
it?
25

26
00:01:24,510 --> 00:01:26,020
So, this is a mask value,
26

27
00:01:26,040 --> 00:01:31,440
and this mask value you have to bitwise | with the data and you get this result
27

28
00:01:31,440 --> 00:01:37,200
and here you can see that these are the unaffected bits, which are not affected at the output.
28

29
00:01:37,200 --> 00:01:37,750
They are safe.
29

30
00:01:38,040 --> 00:01:46,240
So, only 4th and 7th bit positions are set. So, the takeaway from this lecture is this one, bitwise
30

31
00:01:46,300 --> 00:01:54,140
'&' is used to 'TEST' not to 'SET', bitwise '|' is used to 'SET' not to 'TEST'.
31

32
00:01:54,180 --> 00:01:55,850
Now let's do the exercise.
32

33
00:01:55,860 --> 00:01:57,280
So, here is a program.
33

34
00:01:57,330 --> 00:02:05,410
So, I'm actually inputting a number and after that I am actually creating a output by using by bitwise
34

35
00:02:05,480 --> 00:02:07,380
ORing with the mask value.
35

36

So, in this case the mask value is 0x90.
36

37
00:02:11,010 --> 00:02:14,800
So, after that I am just printing the numbers in the hex format.
37

38
00:02:14,910 --> 00:02:16,170
So, let me in enter a number,
38

39

so let's say 56.
39

40

So, this is a result.


━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

video 6

0
1
00:00:00,090 --> 00:00:04,590
So for we covered a testing of bits and setting of bits.
1

2
00:00:04,590 --> 00:00:12,390
So, now let's learn about clearing of bits. So, write a program to clear, a clear means reset that is make
2

3
00:00:12,390 --> 00:00:14,150
bit state to 0.
3

4
00:00:14,250 --> 00:00:16,050
So, write a program to clear
4

5
00:00:16,050 --> 00:00:18,780
4th, 5th, 6th, bit positions of a
5

6
00:00:18,800 --> 00:00:21,490
given number and print the result.
6

7
00:00:21,510 --> 00:00:26,660
So, first of all, which bitwise operation do you use to clear the given bit position
7

8
00:00:26,670 --> 00:00:27,360
of a data?
8

9
00:00:27,360 --> 00:00:28,710
Do you use bitwise
9

10
00:00:28,740 --> 00:00:30,580
& or bitwise |
10

11
00:00:30,660 --> 00:00:34,980
operator? In this case certainly we cannot use bitwise
11

12
00:00:34,980 --> 00:00:43,950
OR (|), because that is used to set. So, you have to use bitwise & operator. So, bitwise '&' is used to
12

13
00:00:43,950 --> 00:00:47,390
'TEST' and 'CLEAR'. So, now
13

14
00:00:47,410 --> 00:00:48,790
let's see how we can do that.
14

15
00:00:48,800 --> 00:00:50,940
Now the goal is to reset
15

16
00:00:50,980 --> 00:00:53,440
4th, 5th, 6th bit position.
16

17
00:00:55,800 --> 00:01:02,640
So, and you should also be careful that the bit positions from 0 to 3 should not be affected,
17

18
00:01:02,760 --> 00:01:06,580
and also the bit position 7 should not be affected.
18

19
00:01:06,630 --> 00:01:13,290
So, what we do is, just select a mask value which helps you to 0 out the required portion.
19

20
00:01:13,320 --> 00:01:17,200
For example, here in this case this portion must be zeroed out.
20

21
00:01:17,250 --> 00:01:25,020
So, that's why, we keep this portion of the mask value as zeroes and these bit positions of the given data
21

22
00:01:25,060 --> 00:01:26,860
should not be affected.
22

23
00:01:26,940 --> 00:01:31,560
So, that's why, let's mask those data portions with once.
23

24
00:01:31,590 --> 00:01:36,860
So, in this case the mask value turns out to be 0x8F.
24

25
00:01:36,960 --> 00:01:41,130
So, this is a mask value. So, that's why, just observe the output
25

26
00:01:41,220 --> 00:01:43,550
so only this portion is zeroed out,
26

27
00:01:43,620 --> 00:01:47,890
so this portion and this portion is not affected.
27

28
00:01:47,940 --> 00:01:54,730
So, the takeaway from this lecture is the bitwise '&' is used to both 'TEST' and 'CLEAR'.
28

29
00:01:54,800 --> 00:01:59,730
Alright. So, there is one more method you can use to clear the bits.
29

30
00:01:59,760 --> 00:02:02,130
This is the method-1 what we just discussed.
30

31
00:02:02,310 --> 00:02:05,120
So here, what you do actually is, here
31

32
00:02:05,140 --> 00:02:12,210
the mask value should be 1 for the unaffected data portion of the data and use zeroes in the mask
32

33
00:02:12,210 --> 00:02:16,290
value 2 zero out the required  bit positions.
33

34
00:02:16,380 --> 00:02:17,290
And here,
34

35
00:02:17,310 --> 00:02:18,460
this is a method-2
35

36
00:02:18,480 --> 00:02:26,490
So, here you use combination of the bitwise & and the negation(~) that is bitwise NOT. So, in this case
36

37
00:02:26,670 --> 00:02:30,730
negate the mask value first and then perform the bitwise
37

38
00:02:30,760 --> 00:02:36,150
&. So here, this is the mask value and you negate that.
38

39
00:02:36,210 --> 00:02:40,020
So, when you negate that the final output is of course this one only.
39

40
00:02:40,320 --> 00:02:46,350
So, but the advantage of this is here you need to think to decide the mask value.
40

41
00:02:46,560 --> 00:02:51,210
So, here you have to work it out and then you have to calculate how much it is.
41

42
00:02:51,300 --> 00:02:55,020
This is actually 8F, but here there is one shortcut.
42

43
00:02:55,020 --> 00:03:01,700
So, for example, in this case I want to zero out 4th, 5th, and 6th bit positions of the data.
43

44
00:03:01,740 --> 00:03:04,110
So, that means 3 bit positions.
44

45
00:03:04,110 --> 00:03:08,970
So, immediately I can guess the number that is 7. So, 7 means
45

46
00:03:09,000 --> 00:03:10,350
111 in binary.
46

47
00:03:10,650 --> 00:03:16,020
So, after that what I do is, I actually left shift this by 4.
47

48
00:03:16,290 --> 00:03:23,220
So, this is bitwise left shift operator. Because, I know that I need to clear out the bits from the 4
48

49
00:03:23,370 --> 00:03:24,480
bit onwards.
49

50
00:03:24,510 --> 00:03:26,720
So, that's why, I left shift by 4.
50

51
00:03:26,910 --> 00:03:32,040
So, we haven't studied about the left shift operator, but don't worry, I will teach you that later.
51

52
00:03:32,040 --> 00:03:37,320
So, this actually gives you a this number and after that you just negate that.
52

53
00:03:37,440 --> 00:03:41,440
So, when you negate that this whole thing will give you this number.
53

54
00:03:41,550 --> 00:03:45,450
So, that's why, the advantage in this method is you need not to think too much.
54

55
00:03:45,690 --> 00:03:50,940
So, that's why, a this method is more convenient, but don't worry if it is confusing you then while doing
55

56
00:03:50,940 --> 00:03:56,950
exercises it will get cleared.To appreciate this method you have to learn the bitwise shift operator as well.
56

57
00:03:57,390 --> 00:04:03,120
So, I hope you understood about clearing of bits, and you can do this exercise, and I'll see you in the next
57

58
00:04:03,120 --> 00:04:03,540
lecture.

0
1
00:00:00,050 --> 00:00:01,320
Hey, welcome back to the lecture.
1

2
00:00:01,320 --> 00:00:06,890
So, the XOR (^) operator you can use to toggle the bits. For example,
2

3
00:00:06,900 --> 00:00:09,050
so let's understand how XOR works.
3

4
00:00:09,060 --> 00:00:13,560
So, you know that XOR operator needs 2 operands.
4

5
00:00:13,590 --> 00:00:20,940
So, let's say operand1 and operand2. The result of this will be 0 when both the operands
5

6
00:00:20,940 --> 00:00:21,480
are equal.
6

7
00:00:21,750 --> 00:00:27,990
So, when they are not equal the result will be 1. That's a truth table of bitwise XOR.
7

8
00:00:28,680 --> 00:00:32,880
So now, you can use this feature to toggle the bits or toggle the data.
8

9
00:00:32,880 --> 00:00:40,620
For example, a let's say here I have a statement Led_state is equal to Led_state ^ with one.
9

10
00:00:40,740 --> 00:00:45,170
So, now let's assume that Led_state is 0, so 0 ^ 1.
10

11
00:00:45,180 --> 00:00:47,270
So, here operands are different.
11

12
00:00:47,310 --> 00:00:49,290
So, the output will be 1.
12

13
00:00:49,320 --> 00:00:51,450
So, now the Led_state is 1.
13

14
00:00:51,450 --> 00:00:54,630
So, 1 ^ 1, Led_state becomes 0.
14

15
00:00:55,020 --> 00:00:56,050
So, like that.
15

16
00:00:56,190 --> 00:00:58,140
So, the Led_state toggles.
16

17
00:00:58,170 --> 00:01:06,270
So, you can use this feature of the XOR to toggle the GPIO, or toggle the LED, or like that when you code
17

18
00:01:06,270 --> 00:01:08,370
for microcontroller program.
18

19
00:01:08,520 --> 00:01:11,360
So, otherwise you have to write the program something like this.
19

20
00:01:11,370 --> 00:01:16,670
So, let's say, there is a while loop while (1). Let's say, if you want to continuously toggle the Led, a let's
20

21
00:01:16,680 --> 00:01:18,660
say you may have to do something like this.
21

22
00:01:18,660 --> 00:01:31,460
If LED is 0, then make LED = 1, else make LED = 0. Suppose, if the Led_state is
22

23
00:01:31,630 --> 00:01:33,390
0, then LED becomes 1.
23

24
00:01:33,400 --> 00:01:36,450
So, if the LED state is 1, then LED becomes 0.
24

25
00:01:36,460 --> 00:01:45,550
So, instead of writing all this code, so you can just replace this by only one line of code that is this
25

26
00:01:45,550 --> 00:01:53,320
one  LED ^= 1. That's it, which is actually used for toggling. Let's use all these operators
26

27
00:01:53,410 --> 00:01:57,280
to write a program for our target board.
27

28
00:01:57,280 --> 00:02:04,330
So, next exercise is write a program to turn on the LED of your target board. For this exercise
28

29
00:02:04,330 --> 00:02:06,840
we need the knowledge of pointers,
29

30
00:02:06,880 --> 00:02:11,680
anyway I have covered the pointers so you should be able to understand this exercise.
30

31
00:02:11,680 --> 00:02:18,100
You should know about bitwise operations, and also you should know about the hardware connections. Because,
31

32
00:02:18,130 --> 00:02:19,690
we are working with the hardware now.
32

33
00:02:19,720 --> 00:02:26,200
We are working with a microcontroller, and we are working with an external component that is LED. So, we
33

34
00:02:26,200 --> 00:02:27,900
have to know the hardware connections.
34

35

So, from the next lecture onwards I will cover all these things and from the next lecture let's start

doing this exercise.

37
00:02:34,130 --> 00:02:35,220
I'll see you in the next lecture.

So, the green LED is connected to the pin PD12, PD12 means Port D PIN number 12, and the LED
32

33

34

PIN number 15.

35
00:02:09,490 --> 00:02:12,020
So now, that leads to our next discussion

36
00:02:12,020 --> 00:02:21,870
What is a port? So now, this microcontroller has many ports and we can identify all this ports by going
36

37
00:02:21,870 --> 00:02:23,220
through the schematic again.

38
00:02:23,340 --> 00:02:24,410
If you go to the schematic,
